OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years
PwBD belonging to General/EWS 10 years
PwBD belonging to OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 13 years
PwBD belonging to SC/ST 15 years
Ex-Servicemen As per Govt. regulations

Persons with 40% or more permanent disability shall only be eligible for relaxations under PwBD category. PwBD candidates must upload Disability Certificate in the format prescribed by Government of India. To avail scribe/reader/seating assistance, separate communication must be sent via https://balmerlawrie.com/feedback within 7 days of application submission.

How to Apply for Balmer Lawrie Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the e-recruitment portal at https://www.balmerlawrie.com/careers/current-openings. No applications sent directly by email or telephone will be entertained. Online submission of application is permitted between 1000 hours on 13.05.2026 till 2359 hours on 05.06.2026. Candidates are advised to complete the application process within official working hours (up to 1800 hours on the last date) as technical support may not be available after 1800 hours.

First-time applicants must register at: Registration Link. After creating your profile, apply against the appropriate position under the "Employment Opportunities" tab. Only creation of profile does not ensure consideration of candidature - candidates must apply against specific positions. Work experience dates must be entered in DD.MM.YYYY format; incorrectly entered data will result in rejection without correspondence.

Documents to be Produced at the Time of Interview (on being shortlisted):

  • Proof of Age
  • Educational Qualifications - all marksheets and certificates (with equivalent % if CGPA/DGPA/letter grade awarded)
  • Service Certificate of past employment with proof of date of joining and continuity in present organization
  • Last Salary Certificate / Pay slips (last 3 months) - where applicable
  • SC/ST/OBC-NCL/PwBD/EWS/ES certificate from Competent Authority (if applicable). OBC certificate must clearly indicate non-creamy layer status.
  • NOC from present employer (where applicable)
  • Past employment proof and present employment proof
  • Identity Proof (Aadhaar / PAN / Passport)
  • Latest passport size photograph

In the absence of any of the above documents, the candidate will not be allowed to appear for interview and no travel expenses shall be reimbursed. Outstation candidates called for interview will be reimbursed travel expenses as per the rules of the Company (mode and class of travel will be intimated in the interview call letter).

Apply Online at: https://www.balmerlawrie.com/careers/current-openings

For Queries (Executive Positions): Email to [email protected] with the Ref. No. of advertisement and position name as subject, or contact 033-22225218. Applications sent directly to the above email shall be summarily rejected.

For General Feedback / Queries (All Notifications): https://balmerlawrie.com/feedback

Important Instructions for Balmer Lawrie Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Candidates must ensure they fulfill the minimum eligibility criteria before applying. The company reserves the right to reject applications at any stage if found ineligible.
  • Applications once submitted cannot be withdrawn. No request for cancellation/change of mailing address/email/category/post will be entertained.
  • All minimum eligibility qualifications should be recognized by UGC/AICTE/Govt. of India/State Govt. and from recognized/affiliated institutes/universities as on the date of passing.
  • Post Graduate Diploma in Management must be certified as equivalent to Post Graduate by AICTE valid for the year when the PG Diploma was completed.
  • ODL (Open and Distance Learning) degrees are accepted only if recognized by UGC; for Management/IT, AICTE recognition is mandatory. Engineering courses through ODL will not be considered (except IGNOU B.Tech for students enrolled up to 2011-12).
  • OBC candidates belonging to "Creamy Layer" are not entitled to relaxation of age and/or reserved positions.
  • OBC-NCL candidates must submit a valid caste certificate issued in the year of advertisement and give a self-undertaking at the time of interview confirming non-creamy layer status.
  • Candidates working in PSU/Govt. Departments must apply through proper channel and produce NOC at the time of interview. Absence of NOC means no pay protection/continuity of service benefit for Executive positions.
  • Selected candidates before joining will be required to undergo Medical examination. If found unfit, they will be debarred from engagement.
  • Any canvassing directly or indirectly by the applicant will disqualify their candidature.
  • Any information or communication regarding changes in the advertisement will be published on the Company's website only. Candidates must regularly check https://www.balmerlawrie.com for updates.
  • All decisions of the Company regarding eligibility, selection, and other matters are final and binding. No correspondence shall be entertained.
  • The court of jurisdiction for any dispute will be at Kolkata.

Candidates who meet the age criteria of up to 40 years and hold a degree in any discipline (Pass Class) from a recognized university can now submit their online applications from 13 May 2026 to 02 June 2026. Direct links to download the official notification and apply online are shared below.

How to Apply for Sarkari Job Online 2026

  1. Find the right Sarkari Job: Use the vacancy table or sector links above on OnlyJobs. Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, or Engineering. Check the last date and eligibility carefully.

  2. Read the official notification: Click on the job listing to open the official notification PDF. Read all eligibility conditions — age limit, educational qualification, experience, category relaxations.

  3. Visit the official recruitment website: Never apply through third-party sites. Use the direct official link provided on OnlyJobs. Look for "Apply Online" or "Register" on the official portal.

  4. Register / Create account: Sign up using your valid email ID and active mobile number. Keep your Aadhaar, bank account details, and email accessible.

  5. Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualifications, category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS), address, and other required information accurately.

  6. Upload documents: Scan and upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, 10th certificate (for DOB proof), educational certificates, caste certificate (if applicable). Check file size and format (JPG/PDF as specified).

  7. Pay application fee: Pay via Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card as applicable. Some categories (SC/ST/PwD/Women) may be exempted from fee. Keep the payment receipt.

  8. Submit and save confirmation: Review all details before final submission. After submitting, download the application printout / PDF confirmation with your registration number. Store it safely for admit card, exam, and future reference.

Why Sarkari Job? Benefits of Government Employment in India

Job Security
Permanent employment — cannot be terminated without due process. Lifelong career stability.
7th Pay Commission Salary
Fixed salary + DA, HRA, TA allowances. DA revised every 6 months. Regular pay revisions.
Medical Benefits
Free or subsidised medical treatment for employee and family under CGHS / state health schemes.
Housing Benefits
Government quarters (where available) or HRA (8–27% of basic pay). No private rent worries.
Pension (NPS / OPS)
Post-retirement monthly pension under NPS or OPS (state-specific). Financial security after service.
Work-Life Balance
Fixed working hours. Paid casual, medical, earned leave. National and state holidays. No overtime culture.
Career Growth
Regular promotions through DPC, ACR-based upgrades, MACP benefits (3 financial upgrades guaranteed).
Children's Education Allowance
Reimbursement of children's school fees (₹2,812/month per child up to 2 children, revised periodically).

Frequently Asked Questions – Sarkari Job 2026

What is Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी)?

Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी) refers to government employment in India — jobs offered by central government departments, state governments, public sector undertakings (PSUs), banks, railways, defence forces, and other government-funded organisations. These jobs offer permanent employment, fixed salary as per 7th Pay Commission, pension, medical benefits, and unmatched job security.

How to find Sarkari Job vacancy 2026 online?

To find online Sarkari Job vacancies in 2026: (1) Visit OnlyJobs daily for updated notifications. (2) Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, PG. (3) Filter by sector — Railway, Banking, SSC, Defence, State PSC, Teaching. (4) Check last dates carefully. (5) Apply through the official department website using the direct link provided. Enable push notifications on OnlyJobs for instant alerts.

What Sarkari Job can I get after 10th pass?

After 10th pass, you can apply for: Railway Group D (RRB), Indian Army Soldier posts, Postal Mail Guard, SSC MTS / Havaldar, Constable GD, Anganwadi Worker/Helper, ITI Apprentice in PSUs (IOCL, HPCL, NTPC), MCL / Coal India posts, and various state government peon/chowkidar posts. Thousands of 10th pass Sarkari Job vacancies open every year.

What is the salary in Sarkari Job 2026?

Sarkari Job salary in 2026 follows the 7th Pay Commission: Group D / MTS: ₹22,000–₹26,000/month. Clerk / Group C: ₹32,000–₹48,000/month. Officer Grade (SSC CGL, Bank PO): ₹47,000–₹85,000/month. IAS/IPS (UPSC): ₹56,100–₹1,44,200 basic pay + DA, HRA, TA, medical and other allowances. State government salaries vary by state pay commission.

Which Bharat Sarkari Job has the most vacancies in 2026?

The largest Bharat Sarkari Job recruitments in 2026 include: Indian Army Agniveer (40,000+ posts), RRB Group D (22,000+ posts), SSC CGL (14,582 posts), SSC MTS (10,000+ posts), UP Police Constable (multiple), Bihar Police (multiple), IBPS Clerk (8,000+ posts), and various state PSC recruitments. Total active sarkari vacancies in India exceed 52,920 as of March 2026.

What is the best website to find Sarkari Job in India?

OnlyJobs is India's most trusted portal for Sarkari Job notifications since 2011, serving over 10 million users. Official direct sources include: ssc.nic.in (SSC), indianrailways.gov.in (Railways), upsc.gov.in (UPSC), ibps.in (Banking), and respective state PSC websites. OnlyJobs aggregates all official notifications in one place with direct apply links.

How to apply for Sarkari Job online in 2026?

To apply for Sarkari Job online: (1) Read official notification PDF. (2) Check eligibility. (3) Visit official recruitment website. (4) Register with email/mobile. (5) Fill application form. (6) Upload photo, signature, and certificates. (7) Pay fee via Net Banking/UPI/Debit card. (8) Submit and lication confirmation. Save the registration number for admit card and future reference.

What are the benefits of Sarkari Job in India?

Key benefits: (1) Lifelong job security. (2) 7th Pay Commission salary + DA revised biannually. (3) HRA (8–27% of basic). (4) Medical benefits (CGHS). (5) Pension under NPS/OPS. (6) Paid leave — casual, earned, medical. (7) Gratuity after 5 years. (8) Children's education allowance. (9) Fixed working hours and work-life balance. (10) Prestige and social recognition in Indian society.

What is the age limit for Sarkari Job?

Age limits vary by post: Group D (18–25 years), SSC CGL (18–32 years), Bank PO (20–30 years), UPSC Civil Services (21–32 years), Defence Agniveer (17.5–23 years). Age relaxations: SC/ST — 5 years, OBC — 3 years, PwD — 10 years, Ex-Servicemen — 3 years (general), Government servants — up to 5 years. Always verify in the official notification for each post.

What is Bharat Sarkari Job?

Bharat Sarkari Job means government job opportunities across the entire country of India (Bharat) — spanning central and state government vacancies across all 28 states and 8 union territories. The term emphasises pan-India coverage. OnlyJobs covers all Bharat Sarkari Job notifications from UPSC, SSC, Railways, Banking, Defence, state PSCs, and all other government departments.

About OnlyJobs 2026

OnlyJobs (Government Jobs) are highly sought-after employment opportunities in India. These positions offer job security, good salary packages, allowances, and retirement benefits. Our platform provides comprehensive information about all types of government vacancies including:

  • Central Government Jobs: UPSC, SSC, Railway, Banking, Defence, PSUs

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is the eligibility for OnlyJobs?

Eligibility varies by position. Generally, candidates need to be Indian citizens aged 18-35 years (with age relaxation for reserved categories). Educational qualifications range from 10th pass to post-graduation depending on the job.

Q2: How to prepare for Government Job exams?

Preparation includes understanding the exam pattern, studying from standard books, practicing previous year papers, taking mock tests, and staying updated with current affairs. Focus on subjects like General Knowledge, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and English.

Q3: Which are the best Government Jobs in 2026?

Top government jobs include IAS/IPS (UPSC), Bank PO, SSC CGL, Railway Group A & B, Defence Services, PSU Jobs, State PCS, and Teaching positions. These offer excellent career growth, job security, and benefits.

Q4: What is the application process for OnlyJobs?

The process typically involves: 1) Online registration on the official website, 2) Filling application form, 3) Uploading documents (photo, signature, certificates), 4) Payment of application fee, 5) Downloading admit card, 6) Appearing for examination, 7) Document verification if selected.

Q5: How often are new OnlyJobs notifications released?

New government job notifications are released daily. Major recruitments like UPSC, SSC, IBPS have annual calendars, while state governments and PSUs release notifications throughout the year. We update our portal daily with 755+ active job listings.

WCD Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – Apply online for the latest Anganwadi Worker, Anganwadi Helper and Supervisor vacancies through the official WCD/ICDS portals such as wcd.nic.in and state ICDS portals. Candidates can check state-wise and district-wise Anganwadi vacancy lists, eligibility, qualifications, age limit, salary, and online application dates, and download the Anganwadi Application Form 2026. Start your career in Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) and stay updated with the latest Anganwadi job notifications from all major states including Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Uttar Pradesh, Andhra Pradesh, Maharashtra, Bihar and more.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Overview

Anganwadi Worker Salary / Honorarium 2026

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers receive a monthly honorarium (not a salary), which includes a Central Government component and an additional State Government component. The table below shows the total honorarium per month for key states in 2026.

State Anganwadi Worker Anganwadi Helper Supervisor

Note: Honorarium figures are approximate and may vary. Refer to official state notification for exact figures. Additional benefits include ESI, provident fund (where applicable), and national health insurance.

Selection Process for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

There is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Helper posts. Selection is merit-based on academic qualifications. For Supervisor and CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted.

How to Apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Online

  • Visit the official portal: Go to your state's official WCD / ICDS website or the direct link provided in the vacancy table above. For central information, visit wcd.nic.in.
  • Find the recruitment notification: Click on the "Recruitment" or "Anganwadi Recruitment 2026" section.
  • Download and read the notification: Download and read the official notification PDF carefully before applying.
  • Register / Login: Create a new account using your mobile number and Aadhaar. If already registered, log in with your credentials.
  • Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualification, address, and select your preferred district/ward. Ensure you apply only for a ward where you are a permanent resident.
  • Upload documents: Upload scanned copies of your passport-size photograph, signature, 10th/12th marksheet, Aadhaar card, and any caste/domicile certificate if required.
  • Review and submit: Check all entries carefully before final submission. Download and print the application confirmation / acknowledgement slip for your records.

  • EWS Certificate (if applicable)
  • PwD Certificate (if applicable)

About this page: Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 information is compiled by the OnlyJobs editorial team directly from official state WCD / ICDS recruitment notifications. We verify all vacancy details, eligibility, and last dates before publishing. Page is updated daily as new notifications are released. For any discrepancy, always refer to the official notification PDF linked in the apply column above.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – FAQ's

How to apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 online?

Visit your state's official WCD / ICDS portal (link provided in table above). Register with mobile & Aadhaar, fill the application form, upload documents (photo, 10th certificate, Aadhaar, domicile), and submit before the last date. Print the application acknowledgement slip after submission.

What is the eligibility for Anganwadi Worker 2026?

Candidates must have passed 10th or 12th standard from a recognized board. Age limit is 18–35 years (varies by state). Must be a permanent resident of the ward/village for which vacancy exists. Only female candidates are eligible for Worker and Helper posts.

What is the salary of Anganwadi Worker in 2026?

Anganwadi Worker receives a monthly honorarium of ₹10,000–₹20,000 depending on the state (Karnataka and TN are highest at ~₹14,000). Anganwadi Helper gets ₹5,000–₹7,000/month. The Central Government pays ₹4,500/month as base honorarium, and states add their own component on top.

Is there a written exam for Anganwadi jobs?

No, there is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts. Selection is purely merit-based on academic qualification marks (10th / 12th percentage). For Supervisor / CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted depending on the state.

What is the age limit for Anganwadi Helper?

Age limit is 18 to 35 years as of 1st January 2026 in most states. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (maximum age 40). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (maximum age 38). PwD candidates get additional relaxation as per state rules.

What documents are required for Anganwadi job application?

Required documents: Aadhaar card, 10th/12th marksheet and certificate, domicile/residence certificate, caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and EWS/PwD certificate if applicable. Graduation certificate is needed for Supervisor post applications.

What is ICDS recruitment 2026?

ICDS stands for Integrated Child Development Services - the government programme under which Anganwadi centres operate nationwide. ICDS recruitment 2026 refers to hiring of Anganwadi Workers, Helpers, Supervisors, and CDPOs by state WCD departments under the Ministry of Women & Child Development (wcd.nic.in).

Is Anganwadi Worker a permanent government job?

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers are honorary workers, not regular government employees. They receive a fixed monthly honorarium (not salary) and are entitled to benefits like ESI, health insurance, and maternity benefits. Supervisors and CDPOs are regular government employees in most states and receive full pay & grade pay.

Can I apply for Anganwadi job from a different district?

No. Anganwadi Workers and Helpers must be permanent residents of the same ward/village/district where the vacancy exists. A domicile or residence certificate is mandatory. Applications from outside the specified locality are rejected. For Supervisor/CDPO posts, the same district residency rule generally applies.

Here you can find state-wise forest department notifications with vacancy, qualification, eligibility, selection process, salary and application last date in one place.

Forest Department Posts List

Forest departments work with a clear hierarchy from senior Indian Forest Service officers to field-level staff like Forest Guard and Forest Watcher. Understanding these posts helps candidates know growth opportunities and career progression in forest services.

  • Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (PCCF) / Head of Forest Force
  • Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (APCCF) / Additional Chief Conservator of Forests
  • Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF)
  • Conservator of Forests (CF)
  • Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) / Divisional Forest Officer (DFO)
  • Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF)
  • Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Range Forest Officer (RFO)
  • Deputy Forest Ranger / Forest Inspector
  • Section Forest Officer / Forester / Van Daroga
  • Beat Forest Officer / Beat Guard / Forester Grade I
  • Forest Guard / Van Rakshak
  • Forest Watcher / Forest Worker / Plantation Worker
  • Forest Ranger (in states where used separately)
  • Field Guard

Most forest department recruitment notifications on this page focus on Forest Guard, Forester, Forest Range Officer, Field Guard and similar Group B, C and D posts.

Eligibility for Forest Department Jobs

Eligibility for forest department jobs depends on the post, state and recruitment authority. Below is a general idea of qualification and age for popular posts like Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer. Candidates should always check the official notification for detailed eligibility criteria.

Education Qualification for Forest Guard, Forester, Ranger

  • Forest Guard / Field Guard / Forest Watcher / Beat Guard: Usually 10th pass or 12th pass from a recognized board, knowledge of local language, and basic physical standards as per state rules.
  • Forester / Section Forest Officer / Deputy Forest Ranger: Generally 12th pass or graduate in any stream or science-related subjects, with physical standards and sometimes driving or technical skills depending on the post.
  • Forest Range Officer (RFO) / Forest Ranger / ACF: Normally graduate degree in Science, Agriculture, Forestry, Environmental Science, Engineering or related disciplines, with higher physical standards and sometimes a competitive exam similar to state PCS level.
  • IFS / Indian Forest Service and senior posts (DCF, CF, CCF, PCCF): Filled through UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination and promotions, requiring a graduate degree and all-India competitive selection.

Age Limit & Relaxation

  • Forest Guard / Forester / Field Guard: Minimum age is usually 18 years and maximum age is around 27–30 years, with age relaxation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-servicemen and other categories as per government rules.
  • Forest Range Officer / ACF and higher competitive posts: Age limit often ranges from 21 to 32 years (varies by recruitment), with relaxations for reserved categories.
  • Exact age limit, cut-off date and relaxation details always depend on the particular notification, so candidates should verify from the official advertisement before applying.

Forest Department Jobs Salary Overview

Forest department salary depends on the post, pay level, state and allowances. Senior officers under Indian Forest Service receive higher pay levels, while Forest Guard, Forester and similar posts fall under state government pay matrices.

Post Pay Level Basic Pay Gross Salary (Approx) Category
Forest Guard / Van Rakshak / Vana Palaka Level 3 ₹21,700 ₹28,000 – ₹35,000 Group C
Forester / Forest Sub-Inspector Level 6 ₹35,400 ₹45,000 – ₹56,000 Group C
Forest Beat Officer / Beat Guard / Junior Ranger Level 4 ₹25,500 ₹33,000 – ₹42,000 Group C
Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Ranger Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹70,000 – ₹90,000 Group B
Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF) Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹75,000 – ₹95,000 Group B

Conservator of Forests (CF) Level 13 ₹1,23,100 ₹1,50,000 – ₹1,80,000 Group A

Additional PCCF (Addl. PCCF) Level 15 ₹1,82,200 ₹2,10,000 – ₹2,20,000 IFS Cadre
Principal Chief Conservator (PCCF / HoFF) Level 17 ₹2,25,000 ₹2,25,000 (fixed) Apex Scale

Selection Process & Exam Pattern

The selection process for Forest Department Jobs 2026 typically includes written examinations, physical tests and document verification. Some posts also conduct medical examination and walking tests due to the demanding nature of field duties.

Written Exam, PET/PST & Document Verification

  • Written Exam: Most Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer recruitments start with a written exam based on objective-type questions (MCQs).
  • PET / PST (Physical Efficiency Test / Physical Standard Test): Candidates who qualify written exam are called for running, walking test, height, chest measurement and other physical parameters as per post and gender.
  • Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates must produce original certificates of age, qualification, caste, residence, sports/NCC and other documents mentioned in the notification.
  • Medical Examination: Many forest posts require medical fitness to ensure candidates can perform field duties in forests, wildlife areas and difficult terrain.

Syllabus Highlights for Forest Guard & Ranger

Typical syllabus for written exam may include:

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs (national, state-level, environment and forest-related topics)
  • General Hindi / English or regional language (grammar, comprehension, vocabulary)
  • General Mathematics / Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10th or 12th level)
  • General Science and Environmental Studies (forest, wildlife, biodiversity, conservation, climate, basic biology)
  • Reasoning Ability (logical and analytical reasoning)

Exact subject-wise marks, number of questions, negative marking and exam duration will be given in the respective forest department notification.

How to Apply for Forest Department Jobs 2026

Most Forest Department Jobs 2026 applications are accepted either online through official websites or offline via prescribed application forms. Candidates should carefully read the official notification before filling the form.

Online Application Process

  1. Visit the official website of the recruiting body (State Forest Department, State PSC, Staff Selection Board or other government portal).
  2. Find the latest link for Forest Guard / Forester / Forest Range Officer / other forest posts and open the detailed advertisement.
  3. Complete the registration process with basic details like name, mobile number, email ID and verify using OTP if required.
  4. Fill the online application form with personal, educational, address and category information exactly as per documents.
  5. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and other certificates in the prescribed format and size.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card or UPI as per your convenience.
  7. Finally, submit the form and take a printout or PDF of the submitted application and payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Required & Application Fee

  • Recent passport size photograph and signature (scanned)
  • Class 10th and 12th mark sheets and certificates
  • Degree / Diploma certificates for graduate-level posts
  • Caste / Category certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, etc.)
  • Domicile / Residence certificate if required by the state
  • NCC, Sports, Ex-serviceman and other quota certificates if applicable
  • Valid ID proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport, etc.)

Why Choose Forest Department Jobs?

Forest department jobs are popular among candidates who love nature, wildlife and field work. These jobs offer job security, government benefits and a chance to work in forest conservation, wildlife protection, eco-tourism and community development.

  • Stable government job with regular salary, pension/NPS and allowances
  • Opportunity to work in forests, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and protected areas
  • Contribution to environment protection, afforestation and biodiversity conservation
  • Good promotion avenues from Forest Guard to Forester, Forest Range Officer and higher posts in many states.

WAPCOS Limited Recruitment 2026: Get the latest WAPCOS recruitment notification 2026 for 159 Expert/Engineer vacancies on Project Term Basis for work relating to various Projects under RDSS (Revamped Distribution Sector Scheme). The application (CV submission) process is open now and the last date to apply is 26th May 2026. Find WAPCOS vacancies on the official website www.wapcos.co.in. You can also get OnlyJobs of "Upcoming WAPCOS RDSS Expert Recruitment 2026" notifications for freshers and experienced candidates. Also know the direct official link for WAPCOS RDSS Recruitment 2026 apply (CV submission) from the WAPCOS career page.

FOLLOW US

WAPCOS RDSS Expert Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

WAPCOS Limited has officially released Advertisement No. WAP/EXPT/TPI-RDSS/2026-27/01 dated 11/05/2026 for engagement of Experts on Project Term Basis for work relating to various Projects under RDSS (Revamped Distribution Sector Scheme). The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, experience requirements, terms & conditions, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification before submitting their CV. The notification PDF is available on the official WAPCOS website.

  • Download WAPCOS RDSS Expert Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

WAPCOS RDSS Expert Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name WAPCOS Limited (A Government of India Undertaking), ISO 9001:2015, Mini Ratna-I
Advertisement No. WAP/EXPT/TPI-RDSS/2026-27/01 dated 11/05/2026
Post Names Assistant Project Manager, Team Leader, Smart Metering Expert, Material Quality Expert, SCADA Expert, Cloud Infrastructure Expert, Cyber Security Expert, Field Engineer, Field Supervisor
Department / Scheme RDSS (Revamped Distribution Sector Scheme) – TPI Projects
Nature of Engagement Project Term Basis (Purely Temporary)
Total Vacancies 159 (variable as per project requirement)
Pay Scale Salary commensurate with qualification & experience; to be discussed at interview
Application Mode CV submission via email only ([email protected])
Selection Mode Shortlisting + Interview
Project Locations Haryana, Chhattisgarh, Andhra Pradesh, Puducherry, Madhya Pradesh, Arunachal Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, and Nagaland
Official Website https://www.wapcos.co.in

WAPCOS RDSS Expert Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Advertisement 11/05/2026
Opening of Application (CV Submission) 11-05-2026
Last Date for CV Submission (Email) 26-05-2026
Age and Experience Calculated As On 30-04-2026
Interview Date To be notified separately
Place of Posting To be finalized at the time of interview

WAPCOS RDSS Expert Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 159 vacancies (variable as per project requirement) have been announced across 9 posts for work under the RDSS scheme. Posts are distributed across expert/managerial and field-level positions in the power distribution sector.

Sl. No. Name of Post No. of Posts
1 Assistant Project Manager 3
2 Team Leader 9
3 Smart Metering Expert 5
4 Material Quality Expert 1
5 SCADA Expert 2
6 Cloud Infrastructure Expert 1
7 Cyber Security Expert 1
8 Field Engineer 46
9 Field Supervisor 91
Total 159

WAPCOS RDSS Expert Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill the qualification and experience requirements as on 30-04-2026. The educational qualification must be from a recognized university/institution. Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/PWBMD candidates shall be applicable as per Government rules. Applications submitted in formats other than the WAPCOS prescribed proforma will be rejected. Self-attested photocopies of all supporting documents (Date of Birth, Qualification, Experience, Category proof) must accompany the application.

Post-wise Educational Qualification and Experience

Post Qualification Experience Required
Assistant Project Manager B.E./B.Tech in Electrical Engineering Minimum 15 years in power Distribution Sector
Team Leader B.E./B.Tech in Electrical Engineering Minimum 8 years in power Distribution Sector
Smart Metering Expert B.E./B.Tech in Electrical / Electrical & Electronics / IT / MCA Total 9+ years in power sector; experience in metering under DDUGJY/R-APDRP/IPDS/Smart Metering projects, AMI/AMU based metering, system metering and energy accounting
Material Quality Expert B.E./B.Tech in Electrical Engineering Total 9+ years in power sector; experience in Quality Inspection under DDUGJY/R-APDRP/IPDS/Saubhagya/Rural Electrification/Loss Reduction/System Strengthening schemes
SCADA Expert B.E./B.Tech in Electrical / Electrical & Electronics / Electronics & Communication / Electronics & Instrumentation / Computer Science / IT Total 10+ years in power sector; experience in UDAY/DDUGJY/R-APDRP/IPDS/Saubhagya; proven Project and Contract Management experience
Cloud Infrastructure Expert Degree in Engineering / IT / Computer Applications from a recognized university 6+ years; experience in IT hardware deployment, cloud hosting services, security audits, cyber security solutions; knowledge of AMI based integration is an added advantage
Cyber Security Expert Degree in Engineering / IT / Computer Applications from a recognized university 6+ years; experience in security audits, risk mitigation, VAPT, designing and implementing Cyber Security policy and solutions; knowledge of AMI based integration is an added advantage
Field Engineer Degree or Diploma in Electrical / Electrical & Electronics Engineering Minimum 3 years post-qualification (Degree) or 6 years (Diploma); experience in power sector Quality Inspection under DDUGJY/R-APDRP/IPDS/Saubhagya/Rural Electrification/Loss Reduction/System Strengthening
Field Supervisor Degree or Diploma in Electrical / Electrical & Electronics / Electronics & Communication / Electronics & Instrumentation / Computer Science / IT Minimum 2 years post-qualification (Degree) or 4 years (Diploma); experience in power sector Quality Inspection under DDUGJY/R-APDRP/IPDS/Saubhagya/Rural Electrification/Loss Reduction/System Strengthening

WAPCOS RDSS Expert Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Age and experience shall be counted as on 30-04-2026. Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/PWBMD candidates is applicable as per Government rules. No specific upper age limit is mentioned in the notification; candidates must refer to the official notification or WAPCOS website for further details.

Category Age Relaxation
General (UR) As per notification / WAPCOS norms
SC / ST / OBC / PWBMD As per Government of India rules
Age Reckoned As On 30-04-2026

WAPCOS RDSS Expert Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

No application fee is mentioned in the official notification for WAPCOS RDSS Expert Recruitment 2026. Candidates are advised to check the official website www.wapcos.co.in for any updates regarding fee.

Category Application Fee
All Categories Not Mentioned / Nil (refer official notification)

WAPCOS RDSS Expert Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for WAPCOS RDSS Expert Recruitment 2026 consists of shortlisting of candidates based on qualifications and experience, followed by an Interview. Place of posting will be finalized at the time of interview. No TA/DA will be paid for attending the interview. The management reserves the right to fix criteria to restrict the number of candidates to be called for interview.

Stage Process Remarks
Stage 1 Shortlisting of CVs Based on qualification and experience criteria
Stage 2 Interview / Interaction Salary and place of posting to be discussed; No TA/DA paid

How to Apply for WAPCOS RDSS Expert Recruitment 2026

Interested candidates must send their CV via email to [email protected] on or before 26.05.2026. The CV must be in the prescribed WAPCOS format only — applications submitted in any other format will be rejected. The subject line of the email should be in the format: CV for TPI-RDSS Project – (Name of Key Position) – (Years of Experience). The prescribed application proforma and further details are available on the official WAPCOS website www.wapcos.co.in.

Documents to be submitted along with CV:

  • Self-attested photocopy of Date of Birth certificate
  • Self-attested photocopies of all educational qualification certificates and marksheets
  • Self-attested photocopies of experience certificates
  • Proof of category (SC/ST/OBC/PWBMD certificate, if applicable)
  • No Objection Certificate / Relieving Certificate (for candidates working in Govt. Departments / PSUs / Autonomous Bodies — required at time of joining)

Original certificates along with CVs are required to be brought at the time of joining for verification. The Company is not responsible for returning any original copies of certificates/testimonials submitted with the application.

Send CV to: [email protected] | For details and format visit: https://www.wapcos.co.in

Important Instructions for WAPCOS RDSS Expert Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Applications must be submitted in the WAPCOS prescribed proforma only; applications in other formats will be rejected.
  • Self-attested photocopies of all supporting documents (Date of Birth, Qualification, Experience, Category proof) must accompany the application.
  • Age and experience will be reckoned as on 30-04-2026.
  • Candidates working in Government Departments / PSUs / Autonomous Bodies must produce a No Objection / Relieving Certificate at the time of joining.
  • Posts are purely on temporary/project term basis as per WAPCOS project requirements.
  • Number of posts is variable and shall be decided as per project requirement.
  • Place of posting shall be finalized at the time of interview; candidates may be posted anywhere in India or abroad.
  • No TA/DA will be paid for attending the interview.
  • The management reserves the right to fix criteria to restrict the number of candidates to be called for interview.
  • The company is not responsible for returning original certificates/testimonials submitted with the application.
  • Subject line of email must follow the format: CV for TPI-RDSS Project – (Name of Key Position) – (Years of Experience).
  • Candidates are advised to visit www.wapcos.co.in regularly for updates.

Note: Reservation as per Government of India norms applicable for SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/EWS/PwBD categories.

RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the crucial date i.e. 01-02-2026. Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply. The educational qualification must be full time and regular course from a recognized University/Institution. Candidates in final year of Graduation/Post Graduation are not eligible to apply.

Educational Qualification (Any One of the Following):

Option Essential Qualification Details
Option 1 Full time & regular B.Sc. (Chemistry) Degree with Physics as one of the subjects + Passing of NCVT examination in Attendant Operator (Chemical Plant) Trade [AO(CP)]
Option 2 Full time & regular Three Years' Diploma in Chemical Engineering/Technology + Successful completion of 1 Year Training (BOAT) under Apprentice Act
Option 3 4 years' / 3½ years' Sandwich Diploma in Chemical Engineering/Technology (Full time & regular)

Minimum Percentage Requirement:

Category Minimum Percentage Required
General/EWS/OBC (NCL) Minimum 55% marks in last year / aggregate of last two semesters of qualifying examination
SC/ST Minimum 50% marks in last year / aggregate of last two semesters of qualifying examination

Important Notes:

  • Only full time and regular courses are acceptable. Distance/Correspondence/Part-time courses are not eligible
  • Candidates in final year of Graduation/Post Graduation are not eligible
  • Reserved category candidates must submit valid Caste/EWS/Disability certificate in prescribed proforma

RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Age limit is calculated as on the crucial date i.e. 01-02-2026. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for reserved categories.

Category Upper Age Limit (as on 01-02-2026)
Unreserved (General/EWS) 30 years
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 33 years
SC/ST 35 years
Ex-Serviceman / Children / Family members of victims of 1984 riots Additional 5 years concession

Note: Age relaxation as per Government of India guidelines. Category once submitted cannot be changed.

RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 - Salary & Allowances

Selected candidates will undergo 6 months training and will receive consolidated stipend during training period. After successful completion of training, candidates will be absorbed in Grade II-(A6) with attractive salary and benefits.

During 6-Month Training Period:

Component Details
Consolidated Stipend Rs. 22,000/- per month
Hostel Accommodation Free of cost (on sharing basis during training)
Medical Facility For self at RCF Hospital
Sports Complex Free access to Sports Complex facility

After Successful Completion of Training (Regular Employment):

Component Details / Amount
Grade & Pay Scale Grade II-(A6): Rs. 22,000 - 60,000
Basic Pay (at minimum of scale) Rs. 22,000/-
VDA (Variable Dearness Allowance) 53.4% of Basic Pay
Perks 34% of Basic Pay
HRA (House Rent Allowance) 30% for Class A cities (subject to company accommodation availability)
Total Monthly Gross Salary (approx.) Rs. 47,800/- at minimum of scale

Additional Benefits:

  • Company accommodation (subject to availability) in lieu of HRA
  • Performance Related Pay (PRP)
  • Free Medical facility for self & dependents
  • Gratuity
  • Contributory Provident Fund (CPF)
  • Accident Insurance
  • Social Security Schemes as applicable

RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay a non-refundable application fee (as applicable). The fee must be paid online only. Fee paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

Category Application Fee
General/EWS/OBC (NCL) Rs. 700/-
SC/ST/Female/Ex-Serviceman candidates NIL (Exempted)

RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 consists of Computer Based Online Test, Document Scrutiny, Skill Test (Qualifying nature), and Pre-Employment Medical Examination. Final selection is based on marks obtained in Online Test only (100% weightage). Skill Test is qualifying in nature.

Computer Based Online Objective Type Test

Particulars Details
Total Number of Questions 100 Questions
Total Marks 200 Marks
Duration 90 Minutes
Type of Questions Objective Type Multiple Choice Questions
Negative Marking Yes (as per IBPS pattern)
Subjects Covered Technical Knowledge, General Awareness, Reasoning, Numerical Ability, English Language etc.

Document Scrutiny:

  • Provisionally shortlisted candidates will be called for Document Scrutiny in the ratio of 1:7 (Vacancy : Candidates)
  • All original documents along with self-attested copies must be produced

Skill Test (Qualifying Nature):

Particulars Details
Total Marks 100 Marks
Minimum Qualifying Marks (General/EWS/OBC-NCL) 60 Marks
Minimum Qualifying Marks (SC/ST) 55 Marks
Nature Qualifying only (marks not counted for final selection)

Final Selection:

  • Final selection is based on marks obtained in Computer Based Online Test only
  • 100% weightage is given to Online Test marks
  • Skill Test is qualifying in nature only
  • Selected candidates must undergo Pre-Employment Medical Examination

Note: Mere fulfilling minimum eligibility criteria does not entitle candidate for selection. RCF Ltd reserves the right to cancel/modify the advertisement without assigning any reason.

How to Apply for RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the official RCFL website (www.rcfltd.com) → HR → RECRUITMENT → APPLY ONLINE or through IBPS portal. No other mode of application will be accepted. The online portal is active from 11-04-2026 to 13-05-2026.

Steps to Apply Online:

  1. Step 1: Visit Official Website - Go to www.rcfltd.com → HR → RECRUITMENT → APPLY ONLINE or directly visit IBPS Portal for RCFL
  2. Step 2: Register - Register with Name, Contact details & Email-id to generate Provisional Registration Number & Password
  3. Step 3: Login - Login using generated Registration Number and Password
  4. Step 4: Fill Application Form - Fill all details in the application form accurately:
    • Personal details
    • Educational qualifications
    • Category details
    • Contact details
  5. Step 5: Upload Documents - Upload scanned copies of:
    • Recent passport-size photograph (as per specifications)
    • Signature (as per specifications)
    • Left Thumb Impression (as per specifications)
    • Hand-written Declaration (as per format given in notification)
    • Required documents in PDF format
  6. Step 6: Pay Application Fee - Pay application fee online (wherever applicable) through available payment modes
  7. Step 7: Submit Application - Review all details carefully and submit the application
  8. Step 8: Take Printout - Take printout of application form and e-receipt for future reference

Important Notes:

  • Keep email ID & mobile number active till completion of recruitment process
  • All communications will be sent via email/SMS only
  • Category once submitted cannot be changed

Documents to be Uploaded at the Time of Online Application:

  • Recent passport-size coloured photograph (as per specifications in notification)
  • Signature (scanned as per specifications)
  • Left Thumb Impression (scanned as per specifications)
  • Hand-written Declaration in English (as per format in notification)

Documents to be Produced at the Time of Document Scrutiny (on being called):

  • Printout of online application form
  • Valid Call Letter
  • Original Photo Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card/PAN Card/Passport/Driving License/Voter ID etc.)
  • Educational qualification certificates:
    • 10th certificate and mark sheet (for date of birth proof)
    • 12th certificate and mark sheet (if applicable)
    • B.Sc. (Chemistry) degree and mark sheets with Physics as subject (Option 1)
    • Diploma certificate and mark sheets (Option 2 & 3)
    • NCVT certificate in AO(CP) Trade (Option 1)
    • BOAT training completion certificate (Option 2)
  • Category certificates (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS) in prescribed proforma issued by competent authority
  • PwBD certificate (if applicable) issued by competent authority
  • Ex-Serviceman discharge certificate (if applicable)
  • Certificate for children/family members of victims of 1984 riots (if applicable)
  • NOC from current employer (for candidates employed in Government/PSU)
  • Any other relevant documents as mentioned in notification

Apply Online at: https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/rcflfeb26/

Important Instructions for RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply
  • Age and qualification criteria calculated as on crucial date i.e. 01-02-2026
  • Candidates in final year of Graduation/Post Graduation are not eligible
  • Only full time and regular courses are acceptable. Distance/Correspondence/Part-time courses not eligible
  • Minimum percentage requirement: 55% for General/EWS/OBC (NCL), 50% for SC/ST in last year/aggregate of last two semesters
  • Category once submitted cannot be changed at any stage
  • Reserved category candidates must submit valid Caste/EWS/Disability certificate in prescribed proforma
  • Candidates employed in Government/PSUs must submit NOC at Skill Test stage
  • Application fee is non-refundable under any circumstances
  • Keep email ID and mobile number active till completion of recruitment process
  • All communications will be sent via email/SMS only - no hard copy will be sent by post
  • Document Scrutiny will be conducted in the ratio of 1:7 (Vacancy : Candidates)
  • Skill Test is qualifying in nature - minimum 60 marks required for General/EWS/OBC (NCL), 55 marks for SC/ST
  • Final selection is based on Computer Based Online Test marks only (100% weightage)
  • Selected candidates must undergo Pre-Employment Medical Examination and must be found medically fit
  • Mere fulfilling minimum eligibility criteria does not entitle candidate for selection
  • Selected candidates will undergo 6 months training and will receive consolidated stipend of Rs. 22,000/- per month during training
  • Hostel accommodation/quarter on sharing basis will be provided free of cost during training period
  • After successful completion of training, candidates will be absorbed in Grade II-(A6) with pay scale Rs. 22,000 - 60,000
  • The number of vacancies is subject to variation based on business requirements
  • RCF Ltd reserves the right to cancel/modify the advertisement without assigning any reason
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification
  • Any dispute arising out of this advertisement shall be subject to the jurisdiction of Mumbai courts only
  • Candidates are advised to visit the official website www.rcfltd.com and IBPS website regularly for all updates regarding exam date, admit card, results, and other notifications

⚡ Key Highlights

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment: Get the latest Indian Air Force recruitment notification for commissioned officer vacancies in Flying Branch and Ground Duty (Technical and Non-Technical) Branches commencing July 2027. Online applications open on 20th May 2026 (1100 Hrs) and close on 19th June 2026 (2330 Hrs). Apply through the official website https://afcat.cdac.in. You can also get OnlyJobs of "Upcoming Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment" notifications for freshers and experienced candidates. Also know the direct official link for IAF AFCAT 02/2026 apply online from the official AFCAT career page.

FOLLOW US

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The Indian Air Force has officially released the AFCAT – 02/2026/ NCC Special Entry / GATE Score Entry notification for grant of Short Service Commission (SSC) in Flying Branch and Ground Duty (Technical and Non-Technical) Branches for courses commencing in July 2027. Online applications are also invited for NCC Special Entry Scheme (for Flying Branch) and Graduate Aptitude Test for Engineering (GATE) Score Entry (for Ground Duty Technical branches). Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification carefully before applying online. The notification and detailed information are available on the official AFCAT website.

  • Download IAF AFCAT 02/2026 Employment News Notification PDF

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment Overview

Organization Name Indian Air Force (IAF), Ministry of Defence, Govt. of India
Notification AFCAT – 02/2026 / NCC Special Entry / GATE Score Entry
Post Name Commissioned Officer (Flying Branch & Ground Duty – Technical and Non-Technical)
Type of Commission Permanent Commission (PC) and Short Service Commission (SSC) for Men & Women
Course Commencement July 2027
Application Mode Online only (through https://afcat.cdac.in)
AFCAT Exam Fee Rs. 550/- + GST (Non-Refundable) | NCC Special Entry & GATE Score Entry: NIL
Pay Scale (on Commissioning) Flying Officer: Rs. 56,100 – 1,77,500/- (Level 10 as per 7th CPC) + MSP Rs. 15,500/-
Official Website https://afcat.cdac.in

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment Important Dates

Event Date
Opening of Online Registration 20-05-2026 (1100 Hrs)
Last Date for Online Registration 19-06-2026 (2330 Hrs)
Course Commencement July 2027

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 - Branch, Course Number and Vacancy Details

Vacancies indicated are tentative and subject to change without notice. Actual intake would depend on organizational requirements, various cadre control mechanisms, availability of training slots, and actual number of vacancies accruing in July 2027. No representation in this regard will be entertained. Two seats are reserved from Ground Duty (Non-Technical) vacancies for Law qualified candidates.

Branch Vacancies
Flying Officer 300+ (Expected)

 

Entry Branch Course Number
AFCAT Entry Flying 222/27F/SSC/M&W
Ground Duty (Technical) 221/27T/PC/, 111AEC/M&W, 221/27T/SSC/, 111AEC/M&W
Ground Duty (Non-Technical) 221/27G/SSC/M&W
NCC Special Entry Flying 222/27F/PC/M and 222/27F/SSC/M&W
GATE Score Entry Ground Duty (Technical) 221/27T/PC/, 111AEC/M&W, 221/27T/SSC/, 111AEC/M&W

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 - Type of Commission

Type Details
Permanent Commission (PC) – Men & Women Candidates joining as PC would continue to serve till the age of superannuation in their respective branches as per their rank.
SSC – Flying Branch (Men & Women) Engagement period for Flying Branch SSC officers is 14 years from the date of commissioning.
SSC – Ground Duty Technical & Non-Technical (Men & Women) Initial tenure for SSC officers in Ground Duty branches would be for a period of 10 years. An extension of 4 years may be granted subject to service requirements, availability of vacancies, willingness, suitability, and merit.
Grant of PC (at a later date) Considered subject to service requirements, availability of vacancies, willingness, suitability, merit, and as per prevailing policies on the subject.

Note: SSC officers are not entitled for grant of pension.

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 - Eligibility Criteria (Age)

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on 01 July 2027. All age calculations are done with reference to 01 July 2027.

Branch / Entry Age Limit
Flying Branch (AFCAT Entry) 20 to 24 years as on 01 July 2027 (born between 02 July 2003 and 01 July 2007, both dates inclusive). Upper age limit for candidates holding valid and current Commercial Pilot Licence issued by DGCA (India) is relaxable up to 26 years (born between 02 July 2001 to 01 July 2007, both dates inclusive).
Ground Duty (Technical & Non-Technical) – AFCAT Entry and Ground Duty (Technical) – GATE Score Entry 20 to 26 years as on 01 July 2027 (born between 02 July 2001 and 01 July 2007, both dates inclusive).

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 - Educational Qualifications

Details on physical/medical standards, educational qualifications, and the full selection procedure are available in the detailed notification on the website. Candidates are advised to visit the official AFCAT website for branch-wise educational qualification requirements.

Branch Educational Qualification (General)
Flying Branch Graduation with Physics and Mathematics at 10+2 level, or BE/B.Tech degree. (Refer official notification for full details.)
Ground Duty (Technical) BE/B.Tech in relevant engineering disciplines. GATE Score Entry candidates must have valid GATE scores in specified disciplines. (Refer official notification for full details.)
Ground Duty (Non-Technical) Graduation in relevant discipline as per the sub-branch (Administration, Logistics, Accounts, Education, Meteorology, Weather Service). (Refer official notification for full details.)

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 - Pay, Allowances & Privileges

IAF Offers the following pay and benefits on commissioning as per 7th CPC:

Rank Pay as per Defence Matrix Level MSP (Military Service Pay)
Flying Officer Rs. 56,100 – 1,77,500/- 10 Rs. 15,500/-

Note: Flight Cadets shall receive a fixed stipend of Rs. 56,100/- per month during one year of training.

Allowances: On commissioning, allowances are applicable based on the branch/nature of posting including Flying Allowance, Transport Allowance, Technical Allowance, Test Pilot & Flight Test Engineer Allowance, Risk and Hardship Allowance, etc.

Privileges: Air Force Officers are entitled to furnished accommodation, comprehensive medical cover for self and dependents, Canteen, Officers' Mess, loans at subsidised rates, LTC, Leave (60 days annual and 20 days casual), subject to service exigencies.

Insurance: Mandatory Insurance Cover is applicable for IAF Officers through Air Force Group Insurance Society (AFGIS). Group Insurance for death while in service is Rs. 1.25 Crore (on contribution). Similarly, PC Officers retiring from service after rendering 20 or more years of service and SSC Officers who have completed 14 years of service are covered under Air Veterans Insurance Cover (AVIC) for Rs. 24 Lacs initially for 15 years, after retirement. The annual transfer for this is Rs. 12 Lacs till the age of 75 years.

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 - Examination Fee

Entry Type Examination Fee
AFCAT Entry (Men & Women) Rs. 550/- + GST (Non-Refundable)
NCC Special Entry NIL (No Examination Fee)
GATE Score Entry NIL (No Examination Fee)

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for IAF AFCAT 02/2026 is merit-based. Candidates registering for AFCAT entry will have to pay the examination fee and appear for the AFCAT Online Test conducted at various centres. Details of conduct of exam, examination centres, and mode of payment are available in the notification.

Stage Process
Stage 1 AFCAT Online Examination / NCC Special Entry / GATE Score Screening
Stage 2 Air Force Selection Board (AFSB) Interview (including Psychological Test, Group Tests, and Interview) at various AFSB centres
Stage 3 Medical Examination at Air Force Central Medical Establishment (AFCME) / Institute of Aviation Medicine (IAM)
Stage 4 Final Merit List and Joining Instructions

Physical Conditioning: Candidates are advised to be physically fit when reporting for SSB. Aim to achieve an ability to run 01 mile (1.6 km) in 10 minutes, do 10 push-ups and 03 chin-ups. Prospective candidates are also advised to keep themselves in good physical condition by training at RFA which encompasses running, swimming, rope climbing, and other forms of physical training/conditioning. Mandatory tests are conducted during training.

How to Apply for IAF AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment

Interested candidates should log on to https://afcat.cdac.in to apply. No other means/mode of application shall be accepted. Candidates are required to possess a valid email ID and active mobile phone number throughout the selection process. Online registration will open on 20-05-2026 (1100 Hrs) and close on 19-06-2026 (2330 Hrs).

Candidates eligible for one or more entries are required to register separately for each entry. After applying online, the candidate is required to download the application slip generated by the system with a unique application number. Information filled in by candidates in the online application form will be considered final and no amendments will be allowed subsequently. Candidates should exercise due care while entering their date of birth.

Important Points Before Registration:

  • Candidates must maintain their unique registered Email ID and mobile phone number active throughout the selection process.
  • PAN Card and account in SBI/Nationalised Bank is mandatory at the time of joining Air Force Academy.
  • Aadhaar Card is mandatory for registration.
  • Candidates must be unmarried at the time of commencement of the course. Marriage during the period of training shall result in discharge and liability to refund all expenditure incurred by the Government.

Apply Online at: https://afcat.cdac.in

For Queries: Related to conduct of online examination, registration process, and admit cards — contact +91 9513252077 or E-Mail [email protected]. For queries regarding eligibility, allotment of AFSB centres, conduct of AFSB interview, merit list, and Joining Instructions — contact 011-23010231 Extn 7610 or Toll free number 1800-11-2448.

Important Instructions for IAF AFCAT 02/2026 Candidates

  • Only Indian Citizens as per Citizenship Act 1955 are eligible to apply.
  • Candidates must be unmarried at the time of commencement of the course.
  • Permanent body tattoos are not permitted on any other body part except on the inner face of forearms (elbow to wrist) and on the reverse side (palm/back dorsal) of both hands. Self-declaration certificate is mandatory for small innocuous tattoos that are not prejudicial to good order and military discipline.
  • Use/possession of narcotics is banned. Candidates found using narcotics at any time during training will be debarred from joining the IAF.
  • Information filled in online application is final — no amendments allowed subsequently. Exercise due care while entering date of birth.
  • Travel Allowance (TA) to and fro for travelling to AFSB will be reimbursed at AC-III Tier/AC Chair Car or actual normal bus fare for first-time candidates. No TA is admissible if you have already appeared for the same type of entry at any of the Selection Boards.
  • Candidates must furnish correct and active Saving Bank Account details (not SB Account like Sukanya Samridhi etc.) for transfer of TA claims.
  • Candidates are advised to keep themselves regularly updated through the official AFCAT website regarding all selection process alerts.
  • Selection in the Indian Air Force is fair and merit-based. Any attempt to influence the selection process at any stage or use of any unfair means like forgery, impersonation, or supplying false information is liable to lead to termination of candidature or initiation of service and legal action against the candidate.
  • Terms and conditions given in the advertisement are only guidelines and are subject to change without notice.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Himachal Pradesh State Co-operative Bank Ltd. (HPSCB) has officially announced the Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 with 91 openings. The detailed notification was published on 09 May 2026 at www.hpscb.bank.in. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 18–45 years (with relaxations for reserved categories) and hold a Graduate degree in any discipline along with the required cooperative society experience can now submit their online applications from 09 May 2026 to 29 May 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below

FOLLOW US

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The Himachal Pradesh State Co-operative Bank Ltd. (HPSCB), Head Office - The Mall, Shimla (H.P.) - 171001 has officially released the detailed notification for recruitment to 91 posts of Junior Clerks (quota for PACS/OCS of HP) on Regular Basis through IBPS, Mumbai. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, selection process, examination pattern, syllabus, PwBD guidelines, fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification before applying online. The notification PDF is available on the official HPSCB website.

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organisation Name The Himachal Pradesh State Co-operative Bank Ltd. (HPSCB), Shimla - 171001
Post Name Junior Clerk (quota for PACS/OCS of HP)
Conducting Body IBPS, Mumbai (Online Examination conducted through IBPS)

These are distributed across General (UR), EWS, SC, ST, OBC, Ex-Servicemen, IRDP, WFF, and PwBD categories. All reserved category benefits apply only to Bonafide Himachali candidates. Number of posts may vary depending on actual requirement at the time of final selection.

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on 01-01-2026. All reserved category candidates must be Bonafide Himachalis. Reservation benefits are applicable only to Bonafide Himachali candidates. OBC candidates must submit a valid Non-Creamy Layer certificate from competent authority. Candidates belonging to SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBDs/Ex-SM and other reserved categories of HP may apply against unreserved/general category posts but will not be eligible for age relaxation or fee concession in such cases. Candidates must also ensure they are not directly or indirectly a defaulter of their society.

Experience certificates must be issued by the concerned Inspector of Society and countersigned by the Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P. with authentic certification. Such certificate must be supported by: (i) Copy of resolution of the managing committee for initial appointment, and (ii) Copy of relevant pages of statutory audit report/note for prescribed years showing payment of wages/salary from the society account.

Educational Qualification

Post / Quota Essential Qualifications
Junior Clerk (PACS Quota - Trained Secretaries of PACS of HP) (i) Graduate in any discipline from a recognized University (Candidates must have passed Matriculation and 10+2 from a School/Institution within Himachal Pradesh - not applicable for Bonafide Himachalis); (ii) Minimum 3 years continuous service as Secretary as defined in Rule 2(vii) of H.P. Cooperative Societies Rules, 1971 of Primary Agriculture Credit Society; (iii) All employees of PACS covered under definition of "Trained Secretaries" are eligible; (iv) Candidate should not be a defaulter of his/her society
Junior Clerk (OCS Quota - Employees of Other Cooperative Societies and PACS employees not covered as Trained Secretaries) (i) Graduate in any discipline from a recognized University (Matriculation and 10+2 from HP school - not applicable for Bonafide Himachalis); (ii) Minimum 5 years continuous service in a cooperative society; (iii) All employees of other cooperative societies and PACS employees not covered under definition of "Trained Secretaries" are eligible; (iv) Candidate should not be a defaulter of his/her society

Types of PACS and OCS covered under this recruitment:

Quota Type Societies Covered
PACS (Primary Agricultural Credit Societies) Cooperative Multipurpose Societies Ltd.; Gram Service Cooperative Societies Ltd.
OCS (Other Cooperative Societies) Fruit Growers and Marketing Society Ltd.; Marketing and Consumer Cooperative Societies Ltd.; Cooperative Consumer Store; Thrift & Credit Society Ltd.; All other Cooperative Societies registered under HP Cooperative Societies Act, 1968

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be above 18 years and below 45 years of age as on 01-01-2026. Candidates born between 02-01-1981 and 01-01-2008 (both days inclusive) are eligible. Age relaxation is applicable only to Bonafide Himachali candidates of the respective reserved categories. Selected PwBD candidates will have to submit a Medical Certificate from a competent authority confirming physical fitness to perform duties.

Category Age Relaxation
General (UR) 18 to 45 years (as on 01-01-2026)
SC of HP 5 years relaxation (max 50 years)
ST of HP 5 years relaxation (max 50 years)
OBC of HP 5 years relaxation (max 50 years)
EWS of HP As per HP Govt. norms
Physically Handicapped (PwBD) 5 years relaxation (minimum 40% disability required)
Ward of Freedom Fighter (WFF) of HP 5 years relaxation
Ex-Servicemen (Reserved Vacancies) Age at time of joining military service should not exceed upper age limit; present age must be less than 58 years (Maximum relaxation: 12 years)
Ex-Servicemen (Unreserved Vacancies) Actual age minus approved military service must not exceed prescribed maximum age by more than 3 years (Maximum relaxation: 12 years)

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates applying for the post of Junior Clerk are required to pay a non-refundable application fee as specified below. The fee must be paid online through the HPSCB application portal. Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances. Bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate.

Category Application Fee
General (UR) and OBC of H.P. Rs. 1,000/- (Rupees One Thousand only) - Non-refundable
EWS of H.P. (Economically Weaker Sections) Rs. 800/- (Rupees Eight Hundred only) - Non-refundable
SC / ST / IRDP / BPL / ANTODAYA of HP Rs. 800/- (Rupees Eight Hundred only) - Non-refundable
Female Candidates of All Categories of HP Rs. 800/- (Rupees Eight Hundred only) - Non-refundable

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 consists of 2 stages - Online Examination (150 marks, 150 minutes, conducted through IBPS Mumbai) followed by Document Verification/Scrutiny. There is no personal interview. Candidates up to 1.75 times of vacant posts will be called for document verification. Final selection will be made strictly on the basis of merit in the online examination after document verification. Candidates must score minimum 40% marks in the online examination to be shortlisted for document verification. Candidates declared successful who do not personally attend document verification will have their candidature cancelled.

Stage 1 - Online Examination (150 Marks, 150 Minutes)

Sl. No. Subject No. of Questions Marks Duration
1 Reasoning 30 30 150 Minutes (2½ Hours) - No separate time limit per section
2 Numerical Ability 30 30
3 English 30 30
4 General Awareness (including HP General Knowledge) 30 30
5 H.P. Cooperative Societies Act and H.P. Cooperative Societies Rules 30 30
Total 150 150  

Stage 2 - Document Verification / Scrutiny

Shortlisting Ratio Candidates up to 1.75 times of vacant posts will be called for document verification
Minimum Qualifying Marks 40% of total marks (i.e., minimum 60 out of 150) in online examination
No Negative Marking There is NO negative marking in the online examination
Question Paper Type Objective type multiple choice (MCQ) - bilingual (English and Hindi)

Tie-Breaking Rules

1st Tie-Break Preference to candidates possessing higher academic qualifications
2nd Tie-Break Preference to candidates belonging to the State of H.P.

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Exam Syllabus

The Online Examination consists of 5 sections - Reasoning, Numerical Ability, English, General Awareness, and H.P. Cooperative Societies Act and Rules - each carrying 30 marks (total 150 marks, 150 minutes). The level of examination and syllabus is in accordance with the minimum educational qualification criteria i.e. Graduation level. The question paper is bilingual (English and Hindi) and is of objective type MCQ. There is no negative marking. Preference will be given to candidates possessing knowledge of customs, traditions, languages, and culture of Himachal Pradesh and the test shall include questions on such subjects.

Section Topic Key Coverage Marks
Section 1 Reasoning Logical Reasoning, Analytical Reasoning, Verbal and Non-Verbal Reasoning 30
Section 2 Numerical Ability Quantitative Aptitude, Number Systems, Arithmetic, Data Interpretation 30
Section 3 English Reading Comprehension, Grammar, Vocabulary, Sentence Completion 30
Section 4 General Awareness (including HP GK) General Awareness, Current Affairs, HP General Knowledge, Customs, Traditions, Languages and Culture of Himachal Pradesh 30
Section 5 H.P. Cooperative Societies Act & H.P. Cooperative Societies Rules Provisions of HP Cooperative Societies Act and HP Cooperative Societies Rules 30

How to Apply for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the Bank's website www.hpscb.bank.in by clicking the "APPLY ONLINE" option. Hard copy applications or applications by any other mode will not be accepted. The online application window is open from 09-05-2026 to 29-05-2026. Candidates must register on the portal, make fee payment online, and upload all required documents. No change will be possible after clicking the "COMPLETE REGISTRATION" button. Candidates are advised to keep their valid personal email ID and mobile number active till the completion of the entire recruitment process.

Before applying, candidates must have ready: scanned photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm), signature (with black ink on white paper), left thumb impression (on white paper with black or blue ink), and a hand-written declaration in English on white paper with black ink. Signature in CAPITAL LETTERS will NOT be accepted. The hand-written declaration text is: "I, _______ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required."

Documents Required to be Produced at Document Verification (when called):

  • Graduation degree certificate and mark sheets from recognized University
  • Matriculation and 10+2 certificates from HP school/institution (if applicable)
  • Experience Certificate countersigned by Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P. - with copy of managing committee resolution for initial appointment and statutory audit report pages (3 years for PACS quota; 5 years for OCS quota)
  • Age proof (10th/12th class mark sheet/certificate)
  • Bonafide Himachali Certificate from competent authority (for reserved category candidates)
  • SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD/WFF/IRDP certificate from competent authority (if applicable)
  • Non-Creamy Layer (NCL) Certificate (for OBC candidates) - in latest prescribed format
  • EWS Income and Asset Certificate issued by Deputy Commissioner/SDO (Civil)/Tehsildar (for EWS candidates) - family income below Rs. 4 Lakhs per annum
  • PwBD Certificate (minimum 40% disability) + Medical Certificate from competent authority confirming fitness for duty (for PwBD candidates)
  • Discharge Certificate (for Ex-Servicemen candidates)
  • WFF Certificate (for Ward of Freedom Fighter candidates)
  • IRDP/BPL/Antodaya Certificate countersigned by Block Development Officer (if applicable)
  • NOC and Good Character Certificate from current employer (for candidates in regular/confirmed service)
  • e-Receipt and printout of online application form

Apply Online at: https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/hpscbmar26/

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Application Link and Form Details

The online application for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 is to be filled and submitted through the Bank's official website. The step-by-step process is as follows:

Step Action Details
Step 1 Visit Bank's Website Go to www.hpscb.bank.in → Click "APPLY ONLINE" → Choose "Click here for New Registration" → Enter Name, Contact Details, and Email ID → Note the Provisional Registration Number and Password generated
Step 2 Fill the Application Form Fill all personal details, category, qualification, and experience details. Use "SAVE AND NEXT" to save data if unable to complete in one go. Details must match exactly as in certificates/mark sheets.
Step 3 Upload Documents Upload: (1) Photograph (4.5×3.5 cm, 20-50 KB, JPG); (2) Signature (black ink, 10-20 KB, JPG); (3) Left Thumb Impression (black/blue ink on white paper, 20-50 KB, JPG); (4) Hand-written Declaration (black ink on white paper, English only, 50-100 KB, JPG). Capture live webcam photo as required.
Step 4 Preview and Complete Registration Click "Preview Tab" to verify all details → Modify if required → Click "COMPLETE REGISTRATION" only after verifying everything is correct. No changes allowed after this step.
Step 5 Pay Application Fee Click "Payment" Tab → Pay online via Debit Card (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard/Maestro), Credit Card, Internet Banking, IMPS, or Mobile Wallets → Wait for server intimation (do not press Back/Refresh) → Download e-Receipt
Step 6 Submit and Print Click "Submit" → Take printout of e-Receipt and online application form with fee details → Download Call Letter (around 7 days before exam) from www.hpscb.bank.in using Registration Number/Roll Number and Password/Date of Birth

For queries: [email protected]

Apply Online at: https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/hpscbmar26/

Important Instructions for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • All reserved category benefits including age relaxation and fee concession apply only to Bonafide Himachali candidates.
  • Candidates must possess valid experience certificate from their cooperative society - false experience certificates will result in summary cancellation of candidature irrespective of qualifying the online examination.
  • Experience certificates must be issued by the concerned Inspector of Society and countersigned by the Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P. supported by managing committee resolution and statutory audit report pages.
  • No hard copy applications will be accepted - online submission only via www.hpscb.bank.in.
  • Application fee is non-refundable; fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.
  • Signature in CAPITAL LETTERS and hand-written declaration not in candidate's own handwriting or not in English will be treated as invalid.
  • Candidates already in regular (confirmed) service must submit NOC and good character certificate from current employer at document verification - absence of NOC will result in cancellation of candidature.
  • There is NO negative marking in the online examination; minimum 40% marks required to qualify.
  • Candidates must appear in person at document verification - non-appearance will lead to cancellation of candidature without notice.
  • OBC candidates must submit a valid Non-Creamy Layer certificate; EWS candidates must have family gross annual income below Rs. 4 Lakhs per annum.
  • PwBD candidates must have minimum 40% degree of disability and must submit Medical Certificate from competent authority confirming fitness for duty at selected post.
  • Wards of Ex-Servicemen are NOT eligible for posts advertised for Ex-Servicemen.
  • Selected candidates will be on probation for a period of two years (extendable up to maximum 3 years).
  • No TA/DA is admissible for appearing in the online examination.
  • Selected candidates will be posted anywhere in the area of operation of H.P. State Cooperative Bank in Himachal Pradesh.
  • For any dispute related to selection process, the decision of the Registrar, Co-operative Societies of Himachal Pradesh shall be binding and final; jurisdiction shall be Shimla (HP) only.
  • Candidates are advised to visit www.hpscb.bank.in regularly for all updates regarding the recruitment process.

Union Bank of India has officially released the notification dated 28.04.2026 for engagement of 1865 Apprentices under the Human Resource Vertical, Manpower Planning and Recruitment Division, Central Office, Mumbai. How to Apply for Union Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the BFSI SSC portal. Hard copy of application and other documents must not be sent to the Bank's office. Candidates should avoid submitting multiple applications; if done unavoidably, only the last valid (completed) application will be retained, and fee paid against other applications will stand forfeited. No change of data is allowed after final submission of the online application.
