  • APAAR ID Card (Format: JPEG, Size: Less than 1 MB)
  • NAPS Apprentice Registration Code and NATS Enrolment Number

Examination fee can be paid by Debit Card / Credit Card / Internet Banking / UPI. On successful payment, an e-receipt will be sent to the registered email ID. Call letters for online examination will be communicated via email from [email protected]. No hard copy of the call letter will be sent by post. For queries: [email protected] or [email protected].

Apply Online at: https://beep.bfsissc.com/candidate_register/mkt

Important Instructions for Union Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Engagement as Apprentice is purely for training and does not guarantee any employment in the Bank.
  • Candidates who have already undergone or are undergoing Apprenticeship training are not eligible.
  • Candidates with more than one year job experience after graduation are not eligible.
  • Only one application should be submitted. Multiple applications will lead to rejection.
  • Proficiency in the local language of the applied state is mandatory.
  • Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.
  • Canvassing in any form will disqualify the candidature.

South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name The South Indian Bank Ltd.
Post Name Junior Officer (Operations)
Recruitment Region Hyderabad and Vijayawada Regions (States of Andhra Pradesh & Telangana)
Type of Employment Contract - initially for 3 years, renewable at Bank's sole discretion based on performance
Career Progression High performing candidates may be considered for regular employment as Assistant Manager (Scale I) after contract period

Eligibility Criteria Calculated As On 31-03-2026

Interview Date To be notified on Bank's official website

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on 31.03.2026. Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. Candidates who have previously attended Group Discussion / Personal Interview for the Junior Officers role as per any previous notification of the Bank and were not included in the final selection list are not eligible to apply for this notification. Proficiency in local language (Telugu) will be given preference. All eligibility is provisional and subject to verification of original documents when a candidate reports for interview/training/joining.

Educational Qualification

Qualification Details
Minimum Qualification Any Graduation with minimum 50% marks from a recognized university.
Stream of Education Regular stream only: SSLC/SSC, HSC and Graduation (10+2+ Graduation under regular stream).
Work Experience Minimum 1 year of experience in Bank / NBFCs.
Special Note Candidates who have attended Group Discussion / Personal Interview for Junior Officers role as per previous notification(s) and were not included in the final selection list are NOT eligible to apply.

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

The maximum age limit is 28 years as on 31.03.2026. Age relaxation of 5 years is available for SC/ST candidates. Candidates belonging to SC/ST must submit attested copies of certificates from a competent authority to prove their status as and when called for.

Category Age Limit
General (UR) Maximum 28 years (as on 31.03.2026)
SC / ST 5 years of age relaxation (maximum 33 years)

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

No application fee is required for this post. Candidates can apply free of cost through the official South Indian Bank recruitment portal.

Category Recruitment Fee
All Categories NIL (No Application Fee)

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Terms of Employment

Selected candidates will be appointed on a contract basis initially for a period of 3 years, renewable at the sole discretion of the Bank based on performance evaluation. On completion of the initial contract period or any extension, high performing candidates may, without any obligation and in the Bank's sole and absolute discretion, be considered for regular employment as Assistant Manager (Scale I), subject to the then prevailing norms of the Bank. There will be no mandatory minimum service period as stipulated in the case of staff members recruited under regular employment.

Parameter Details
Designation / Job Role Junior Officer (Operations)
Term of Appointment Contract - initially 3 years, renewable at Bank's sole discretion based on performance
Career Progression High performers may be considered for regular employment as Assistant Manager (Scale I) after contract period, at Bank's sole discretion
Posting Location Anywhere in Andhra Pradesh & Telangana as per Bank's requirement; liable to be transferred anywhere in India at Bank's sole discretion
Total CTC Rs. 4.86 lakhs to Rs. 5.04 lakhs per annum (inclusive of NPS contribution, Insurance Premium and Variable Pay based on performance)
Official Duty Allowance Eligible Lodging, Halting and Travelling allowance for official travel, as per Bank norms
Insurance Group Medical Insurance and Group Accident Insurance at the cost of the Bank; Group Life Insurance with premium borne by the employee
Increment Annual increment during contract period based on performance evaluation or other parameters as decided by the Bank

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 consists of an Online Test and/or Group Discussion, followed by a Preliminary and/or Final Interview. The Online Test and Group Discussion are elimination stages - only test-qualified candidates will be called for Group Discussion or Personal Interview, and only candidates shortlisted from the Group Discussion will be advanced to the Personal Interview. The Bank reserves the right to modify the selection process based on the number of applications received. Mere eligibility will not vest any right on the applicant to be called for the Recruitment Process. All marks/scores/grades at any stage shall be treated as strictly confidential.

Stage Details Nature
Stage 1 Online Test (if conducted) Elimination Stage - conducted at locations identified by the Bank
Stage 2 Group Discussion (if conducted) Elimination Stage - only Online Test qualified candidates called
Stage 3 Preliminary and/or Final Personal Interview Only Group Discussion shortlisted candidates advanced

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 Online Application Form

Eligible candidates who wish to apply for the South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) posts can do so by submitting their application form online. The application process is online only through the official South Indian Bank recruitment portal, and candidates must ensure that they complete all steps before the closing date.

The application form for the South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) Recruitment 2026 is available on the official South Indian Bank recruitment website at https://recruit.southindianbank.bank.in/RDC/. Eligible candidates with any Graduation (minimum 50% marks) and minimum 1 year of experience in Bank/NBFCs are encouraged to fill out the online application form and upload the required documents before the last date.

Online Apply Link for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026

How to Apply for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026

Applicants can apply online only through the Bank's official recruitment website at https://recruit.southindianbank.bank.in/RDC/ from 06-05-2026 to 13-05-2026. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before registering, applicants must ensure they have a valid Email ID and mobile number in their name. An applicant can only apply for vacancies in one State - making multiple registrations will result in disqualification.

There is no provision to modify the submitted online application - candidates are requested to fill in the application form with utmost care. After successful submission, a system generated User ID (Application Ref. ID) will be generated - candidates must note this for future reference. An email with the application PDF and login credentials will be sent to the registered email ID upon successful submission. The physical copy of the application need not be sent to the Bank.

Documents / Upload Specifications:

  • Photograph: Recent passport size colour photo, white background, no caps/hats/dark glasses - Resolution: 378 x 437 pixels, maximum 50 KB (JPEG/JPG format)
  • Signature: Signed on white paper with black ink pen - Resolution: 140 x 110 pixels, maximum 50 KB
  • Resume: PDF format, file size not exceeding 1 MB
  • Education Documents: Certificates and marklists of X/SSLC (must include D.O.B), XII/HSC and final marklist and certificate of Graduation - combined as a single PDF file, not exceeding 3 MB

Documents to be Produced When Called for (Originals):

  • Proof of age (SSLC/SSC certificate with Date of Birth)
  • Educational qualification certificates and marklists (X, XII, Graduation)
  • Work experience certificate(s) proving minimum 1 year in Bank/NBFCs
  • SC/ST certificate from competent authority (if applicable)
  • Proof of nationality / domicile as applicable
  • Any other documents as required by the Bank

For queries, contact: Toll Free: 1800-425-1809 / 1800-102-9408, 0484-277-1383 | Email: [email protected]

Apply Online at: https://recruit.southindianbank.bank.in/RDC/

Important Instructions for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Beware of fraudulent job alerts - apply only through the official online portal. Applications through any other mode will not be accepted.
  • Candidates who have previously attended Group Discussion / Personal Interview for Junior Officers role as per any previous notification and were not included in the final selection list are NOT eligible to apply.
  • An applicant can apply for vacancies in only one State - multiple registrations will lead to disqualification.
  • There is no provision to modify the submitted online application - fill in all details carefully before submission.
  • No application fee is required for this post.
  • Appointment is subject to Medical fitness, satisfactory background verification including CIBIL/credit history check, police clearance, and completion of other formalities as per Bank rules.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Applicants must appear for the Recruitment Process at their own cost.
  • Call letters/advice will be sent by email only - no hard copy will be sent. Candidates are advised to check their registered email and the Bank's official website regularly.
  • All marks/scores/grades obtained at any stage of the selection process are strictly confidential and will not be disclosed to any party including candidates.
  • Applicants are advised to avoid last-minute rush and submit the online application well before the closing date, as website access may be delayed due to heavy internet traffic near the deadline.
  • The Bank reserves the right to make modifications to the selection process and the qualifying criteria at any stage, and such decisions will be final and binding.

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Overview

Field Details
Commission/Organization Name Reserve Bank of India Services Board (RBISB), Mumbai
Advertisement No. RBISB/DA/01/2026-27
Post Name Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) (On Probation) – General / DEPR / DSIM Cadres

Phase-II Exam – DEPR / DSIM Cadre 26-07-2026

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 60 vacancies have been announced across three cadres – General, DEPR, and DSIM. Vacancies are distributed across GEN/UR, EWS, OBC, SC, and ST categories.

Post Total
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – General Cadre 40
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DEPR Cadre 10
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DSIM Cadre 10
Total 60

Sr. No. Post PwBD Category A PwBD Category B PwBD Category C PwBD Category D
1 Grade 'B' (DR) – General – – 1 1

3 Grade 'B' (DR) – DSIM 1 – – –

Category A: Blindness & Low Vision; B: Deaf & Hard of Hearing; C: Locomotor disability; D: Autism, intellectual disability, specific learning disability and mental illness

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on April 01, 2026. Nationality conditions apply – the post is open to citizens of India and certain other categories as specified in the notification, subject to eligibility certificate from the Government of India where required.

The educational qualification must be from a University incorporated by an Act of the Central or State Legislature, or an institution declared Deemed University under UGC Act, 1956, or an equivalent foreign university recognized by the Association of Indian Universities.

Educational Qualification

Post Minimum Educational Qualifications (as on April 01, 2026)
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – General Graduation in any discipline with minimum 60% marks (50% for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved for them) OR Post-Graduation with minimum 55% marks (pass marks for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved for them) in aggregate of all semesters/years.
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DEPR Essential: MA/MSc in Economics or related Economics/Finance disciplines with minimum 55% marks (50% for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved) from a recognized Indian or Foreign University. Desirable: Doctorate in Economics, or research/teaching experience, or publication in standard journals.
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DSIM Essential: Master's Degree with minimum 55% marks in Statistics/Mathematics/Data Science/AI/ML or related fields OR Four-year Bachelor's Degree with minimum 60% marks in the above fields (50% for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved). Desirable: Doctorate in related subjects; research/teaching experience and publications.

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must have attained the age of 21 years and must not have attained the age of 30 years as on April 01, 2026 (born not earlier than April 02, 1996 and not later than April 01, 2005). The upper age limit is relaxed for various categories as detailed below.

General/EWS candidates who have already appeared six times for Phase-I Examination for this post in the past are not eligible to apply. No such restriction applies to SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates if posts are reserved for them.

Category Age Limit
General (UR) / EWS 21 to 30 years (as on 01-04-2026)
SC / ST 5 years relaxation (max 35 years)
OBC 3 years relaxation (max 33 years)
PwBD – Gen/EWS 10 years relaxation (max 40 years)
PwBD – OBC 13 years relaxation (max 43 years)
PwBD – SC/ST 15 years relaxation (max 45 years)
Ex-Servicemen / Ex-Bank Employees 5 years relaxation
Candidates with M.Phil. Upper age limit 32 years
Candidates with Ph.D. Upper age limit 34 years
Bank Officers (experience-based relaxation) Up to 3 years relaxation; upper age not to exceed 33 years including experience
DEPR/DSIM – Master's with Research/Teaching Experience Up to 3 years relaxation

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates applying for the post of Officers in Grade 'B' are required to pay a non-refundable fee/intimation charges as specified below. The fee must be paid online through the RBI application portal. Fee paid will not be refunded under any circumstances nor held in reserve for any other examination or selection.

Candidates submitting applications without the prescribed fee/intimation charges will be summarily rejected. Candidates are therefore advised to confirm their eligibility and verify all details before making payment.

Category Charges Amount
SC / ST / PwBD Intimation Charges only ₹100/- + 18% GST
GEN / OBC / EWS Application Fee including Intimation Charges ₹850/- + 18% GST
RBI Staff (eligible Staff Candidates) Nil Nil

Bank/Transaction charges are to be borne by the candidate.

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for RBI Grade B Recruitment 2026 consists of Phase-I Online Examination, Phase-II Online/Written Examination, and Interview. Candidates who qualify Phase-I will be shortlisted for Phase-II based on aggregate marks. Only those who clear Phase-II will be called for the Interview.

No candidate will be called for the Interview unless he/she appears in Phase-II examination. The final merit list will be prepared by adding marks secured in Phase-II and Interview (and grace marks as applicable). If a candidate remains absent in the Interview, his/her candidature will not be considered for final selection.

The selection list recommended by the Board remains valid for the period as decided by the Board/Bank.

Stage 1 – Phase-I Online Examination (Grade 'B' DR – General)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Single Paper General Awareness, English Language, Quantitative Aptitude, Reasoning Objective MCQ 200 120 Minutes

Stage 1 – Phase-I Online/Written Examination (DEPR Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Economics – Objective Type Online MCQ 100 120 Minutes
Paper-II English – Descriptive Typed on keyboard 100 120 Minutes

Stage 1 – Phase-I Online Examination (DSIM Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Statistics – Objective Type Online MCQ 100 120 Minutes

Stage 2 – Phase-II Online Examination (Grade 'B' DR – General)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Economic and Social Issues 50% Objective + 50% Descriptive 100 120 Minutes
Paper-II English (Writing Skills) Descriptive (typed) 100 90 Minutes
Paper-III General Finance and Management 50% Objective + 50% Descriptive 100 120 Minutes
Grand Total     300  

Stage 2 – Phase-II Written Examination (DEPR Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Economics – Descriptive Pen/Paper (questions on screen) 100 120 Minutes
Paper-II Economics – Descriptive Pen/Paper (questions on screen) 100 120 Minutes
Total     400 (incl. Phase-I)  

Stage 2 – Phase-II Written Examination (DSIM Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-II Statistics – Descriptive Pen/Paper (questions on screen) 100 180 Minutes
Paper-III English – Descriptive Typed on keyboard 100 90 Minutes
Total     300 (incl. Phase-I)  

Stage 3 – Interview: 75 Marks (All Cadres)

Candidates will also undergo a Personality Assessment before the Interview. No marks are allotted for Personality Assessment and it does not form part of the final selection criteria. Candidates may opt for Interview in Hindi or English.

Qualifying Marks

Category Minimum Qualifying Marks
All Categories Minimum marks separately for each test/paper as prescribed by the Board; minimum aggregate cut-off for Phase-II shortlisting decided by the Board in relation to number of vacancies

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Exam Syllabus

Phase-I – General Cadre (200 Marks, Objective MCQ, 120 Minutes)

The paper covers General Awareness, English Language, Quantitative Aptitude, and Reasoning. Separate time will be allotted for each section. Detailed information will be provided in the Information Handout available with the Admit Card.

Phase-II – General Cadre

Paper-I (Economic and Social Issues): Growth and Development (National Income, poverty alleviation, sustainable development); Indian Economy (economic history, monetary and fiscal policy since 1991, Indian money and financial markets, public finance, agriculture, services sector); Globalization (BoP, export-import policy, IMF, World Bank, WTO); Social Structure in India (multiculturalism, demographic trends, urbanization, gender issues, social justice).

Paper-II (English – Writing Skills): Writing skills including expression and understanding of the topic.

Paper-III (General Finance and Management): Financial system (structure of financial institutions, RBI functions, banking system, digital payments); Financial markets (primary and secondary markets); General topics (financial risk management, derivatives, financial inclusion, corporate governance, Union Budget, accounting basics, inflation); Management (fundamentals, organizational behaviour, motivation, leadership, ethics, corporate governance, communication).

Phase-I – DEPR Cadre (Paper-I, Objective – Economics)

Microeconomics, Macroeconomics (IS-LM Model, schools of economic thought), International Economics, Theories of Economic Growth and Development, Public Finance, Environmental Economics, Quantitative Methods in Economics, Current developments in Indian Economy.

Phase-II – DEPR Cadre (Descriptive – Economics)

Paper-I covers Microeconomics Module (consumer theory, production theory, market theory, welfare economics) and Macroeconomics Module (national income accounting, employment and output, inflation, money and banking, growth theories, international trade, public finance) with equal weightage. Paper-II covers Quantitative Methods in Economics (mathematical methods, statistical methods, econometrics) and Indian Economy – Policy and Trends (fiscal policy, monetary policy, banking sector, inflation, external sector, sectoral developments) with equal weightage.

Phase-I – DSIM Cadre (Paper-I, Objective – Statistics) and Phase-II (Paper-II, Descriptive – Statistics)

Topics covered across both papers: Theory of Probability, Probability Distributions and Sampling Theory; Linear Models and Economic Statistics; Statistical Inference (Estimation, Testing of Hypothesis, Non-Parametric Tests); Stochastic Processes; Multivariate Analysis; Econometrics and Time Series; Optimization and Statistical Computing; Data Science, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Techniques; Database and Data Warehouse Management.

How to Apply for RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the Bank's website www.rbi.org.in. No other means/mode of application will be accepted. The online portal will be active from 29-04-2026 to 20-05-2026 (6:00 PM). Candidates desirous of applying for both Grade 'B' (DR)-General and Grade 'B' (DR)-DEPR or DSIM must apply using separate links and pay fees for both. Digi Locker has been integrated with the online application form for voluntary submission of credentials.

Candidates should avoid submitting multiple applications; if done unavoidably, only the application with the higher Registration ID (RID) will be considered valid, and fee paid against other RIDs will not be adjusted. After the closing date, a 2-day edit window will be available for making corrections in limited fields. Candidates are not required to upload supporting documents at the time of online application; documents will be verified at the Interview stage only.

Documents Required at Interview Stage:

  • Age proof (birth certificate or Class X marksheet/certificate)
  • Educational qualification certificates and marksheets
  • SC / ST / OBC / PwBD / EWS / Ex-SM certificate (if applicable) in prescribed format
  • Photo identity proof (PAN Card / Passport / Driving Licence / Voter's Card / Aadhaar, etc.)
  • Online application form printout with fee payment receipt

Candidates working in Government/PSU/Public Enterprises must inform their employer in writing before applying and submit an undertaking to this effect at the time of online application. At the time of joining, proper discharge certificates from their employer must be produced.

Apply Online at: https://www.rbi.org.in

Important Instructions for RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only eligible citizens (as defined in the notification) are eligible to apply; nationality certificate required for certain categories.
  • Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on April 01, 2026.
  • No hard copy applications will be accepted – online submission only.
  • Recruitment fee/intimation charges are non-refundable; applications without prescribed fee will be summarily rejected.
  • Government/PSU employees must submit an undertaking about informing their employer and bring discharge certificate at the time of joining.
  • Mobile phones, pagers, and all electronic devices are banned inside examination/interview premises; physical calculators are not permitted in any phase.
  • Candidates reporting after Gate Closure Time specified on the Admit Card will not be permitted to take the examination.
  • Candidates are advised to visit https://www.rbi.org.in regularly for updates; all corrigenda will be published on the Bank's website only.
  • Phase-I paper (General Cadre) will be set in English; all other papers (except English papers) will be set bilingually in Hindi and English.
  • Negative marking on MCQ questions will apply as prescribed; detailed marking scheme will be in the Information Handout.
  • For any problem in filling the form or downloading Admit Card, queries may be raised through http://cgrs.ibps.in mentioning the post and cadre in the subject line.
  • All decisions of the Board regarding eligibility, conduct of examinations, selection, and other matters are final and binding; any resultant dispute is subject to sole jurisdiction of Courts at Mumbai.

UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Age Limit 2026

The age limit to apply for UPCISB Clerk, Junior Manager and More Posts Recruitment 2026 is 21 to 40 years. Age relaxation will be provided to reserved category candidates as per government rules.

UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Application Fee 2026

  • Application fee is to be paid only through online mode via debit card, credit card, internet banking, UPI, IMPS, cash card or mobile wallet during 25 April 2026 to 15 May 2026.
  • Bank transaction charges for payment of application fee will be borne by the candidate and the fee once paid is non-refundable.
Category Application Fee
Unreserved (General) / OBC / EWS Rs. 500/-
Scheduled Caste (SC) / Scheduled Tribe (ST) Rs. 250/-

UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Selection Process 2026

  • The selection process consists of an online Preliminary Examination (if required) and a Main Examination conducted by UPCISB.
  • Based on the Preliminary Exam, up to 15 times the number of vacancies will be shortlisted for the Main Exam.
  • On the basis of Main Exam result, up to 3 times the number of posts will be called for interview (where applicable) and up to 5 times the number of posts for proficiency test, wherever prescribed.
  • Both Preliminary and Main examinations will be objective-type online tests with multiple choice questions and negative marking of 1/4 mark for each wrong answer.

How to Apply for UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Recruitment 2026

  • Candidates must apply online only through the official website of U.P. Co-operative Institutional Service Board: https://www.upcisb.upsdc.gov.in.
  • First, complete “New Registration” by entering name, contact details and email ID to generate a registration number and password.
  • Fill in the online application form carefully using the “Save and Next” option, upload scanned photograph, signature, left-hand thumb impression and handwritten declaration as per the prescribed specifications.
  • Pay the application fee through the integrated payment gateway, ensure e-Receipt generation, and take a printout of the final submitted application form and fee receipt for future reference.

UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Salary 2026

  • The notification provides detailed pay scales for each post, such as specific minimum and maximum pay in the respective pay bands for Manager, Junior Manager, Assistant/Cashier, Assistant Manager, Accountant, Junior Engineer, Assistant Engineer and other posts.

  • Pay scales differ institution-wise and post-wise (for example, different pay bands for Junior Manager in different District Co-operative Banks), and all posts are permanent with regular pay as per the respective co-operative institution’s rules.

Important Instructions for Candidates

  • Candidates must thoroughly read and understand all instructions in the advertisement before filling the online application form.
  • The photograph, signature, thumb impression and handwritten declaration must be clear and as per the prescribed size and format, otherwise the application may be rejected.
  • No change is permitted in key details after clicking on “Complete Registration”, so all information (name, father’s name, etc.) must match official certificates.
  • Candidates should keep their registered email ID and mobile number active till completion of the recruitment process for receiving admit card and other communication from UPCISB.
  • Minimum efficiency standard (qualifying marks) and negative marking rules in the exam must be noted carefully, as incorrect answers will attract penalty.

How to Raise Objection Against SBI Clerk Mains Answer Key 2026 ?

If you disagree with any answer (check if objection facility is provided):

  2. Go to the relevant section for challenge/objection (if available)
  3. Select the question(s) you want to challenge
  4. Upload supporting documents or references (if required)
  5. Pay the objection fee (if applicable, refundable if accepted)
  6. Submit before the deadline (check portal for exact date)

How to Calculate Your Score Using SBI Clerk Mains Answer Key 2026 ?

Use this formula to estimate your score:

Total Score = (Correct Answers × Marks per Question) – (Wrong Answers × Negative Marking)

Marking Scheme for SBI Clerk Mains Exam:

  • Correct Answer: +1 mark (most sections), +2 marks for Computer Aptitude
  • Wrong Answer: -1/4 mark (negative marking)
  • Unattempted: 0 marks

South East Central Railway (SECR), Personnel Department, Divisional Railway Manager Office, Bilaspur has officially announced the Act Apprentice (Trade Apprentice) - Various Trades Recruitment 2026 with 1191 openings. The detailed notification was published on 12 May 2026 at secr.indianrailways.gov.in. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 15 to 24 years (relaxation applicable for SC/ST/OBC/PWD/Ex-Servicemen) and hold Matriculation (10th) certificate and ITI in the relevant trade can now submit their online applications from 12 May 2026 to 11 June 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below.

FOLLOW US

South East Central Railway SECR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The Personnel Department, Divisional Railway Manager Office, Bilaspur Division, South East Central Railway has officially released Notification No. P/विलासपुर/भर्ती/एक्ट अप्रेंटिस/2026-27/E-176508 dated 12-05-2026 for engagement of 1191 Act Apprentices in Bilaspur Division for the year 2026-27 under the Apprentices Act 1961 and Apprenticeship Rules 1962. The notification includes complete details on vacancies (trade-wise), eligibility, age limit, apprenticeship period, stipend, merit criteria, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to carefully read the full notification before applying online. The notification is available on the official Apprenticeship India portal.

  • Download SECR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 Official Notification PDF

SECR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 1191 vacancies have been announced trade-wise across UR, EWS, OBC, SC and ST categories for Bilaspur Division. Ex-Servicemen reservation is 10% (horizontal, included in respective parent categories). PWD reservation is horizontal and included in overall vacancies. Track Machine/BSP total is 17 (all categories: 0 notified separately; included in total).

SECR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the last date of application, i.e. 11-06-2026. Only 10th and ITI marks should be filled in the qualification section on the portal — no other higher qualification should be filled, otherwise the application will be automatically rejected. If an applicant belongs to SC/ST/OBC community, they should upload a recent caste certificate issued by the competent authority on the portal.

Educational / Technical Qualification

Qualification Details
Academic Must have passed 10th class examination under 10+2 system or its equivalent.
Technical Must have passed ITI course in relevant trades from a recognized institution.

SECR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

The candidate should have completed 15 years of age and should not have completed 24 years of age as on 11-06-2026. Age relaxation is subject to submission of latest caste certificate. Date of birth community-wise is as under:

Srl. No. Community Date of Birth (both dates inclusive)
1 UR 11-06-2002 to 11-06-2011
2 SC 11-06-1997 to 11-06-2011
3 ST 11-06-1997 to 11-06-2011
4 OBC 11-06-1999 to 11-06-2011
5 PWD / Ex-Servicemen 11-06-1992 to 11-06-2011

Upper age limit is relaxable by 05 years for SC/ST candidates, 03 years for OBC, and 10 years for Ex-Servicemen and PWD.

SECR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection will be based on a merit list prepared by taking the average of the percentage marks obtained by candidates in both Matriculation (with minimum 50% aggregate marks) and ITI examination, giving equal weightage to both (Establishment Rule 201/2017). Candidates must fill their 10th and ITI marks on the portal in the qualification section; otherwise the application will be automatically rejected. No higher qualification should be filled. There is no written examination — selection is purely on merit basis.

Merit Criteria Details
Basis Average of % marks in Matriculation (min. 50% aggregate) and ITI, with equal weightage to both
Medical Selected candidates may be asked to bring a medical certificate at the time of document verification (signed by Government authorized Doctor Gazetted, not below rank of Asstt. Surgeon of Central/State Hospital)
Contract Selected candidates (or guardian if minor) must enter into a contract of apprenticeship with the employer

Apprenticeship Period & Stipend

Apprenticeship Duration 1 year for each trade
Stipend As per rules of State Government of Chhattisgarh (paid during training period)
Post-Training Obligation It is not obligatory for the employer to offer employment after training completion, nor for the apprentice to accept any employment, if offered. Training will be terminated after completion of apprenticeship.

How to Apply for SECR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the website https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in from 12-05-2026 to 11-06-2026 (till 23:59 hrs). No physical copy of the application is required to be sent to the office. Candidates must upload a soft/scanned copy of their present passport size color photograph and scanned signature along with necessary documents pertaining to educational/technical qualification on the website. Candidates are advised to apply online well before the closing date.

If the option of EWS/Ex-Serviceman is not available on the portal, candidates should apply on the portal from their actual category (e.g. UR, OBC, SC, ST) and send/submit the EWS/Ex-Serviceman certificate physically to this office.

Note: Applicants whose father’s name and applicant’s name in their 10th mark sheet, ITI mark sheet, Aadhar card and PAN card do not match, their application will be rejected at the time of document verification. In case of any doubt/contradiction, only the English format of the notification will be valid.

Apply Online at: https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in

Important Instructions for SECR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Applications must be submitted online only on https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in; no physical copy is required to be sent to this office.
  • Candidates must fill only their 10th and ITI marks on the portal in the qualification section — do not fill any other higher qualification, otherwise the application will be automatically rejected.
  • Candidates belonging to SC/ST/OBC community must upload a recent caste certificate issued by the competent authority on the portal.
  • PWD candidates must produce a disability certificate issued by a competent authority with not less than 40% of disability (Medical Board duly constituted by Central or State Government).
  • Ex-Servicemen candidates selected under the 10% reservation will be placed in the appropriate category (UR/SC/ST/OBC) to which they belong.
  • Candidature will be cancelled if the candidate fails to produce required original testimonials for verification or any discrepancy is noticed.
  • If wrong/fake/false certificates are noticed, the railway administration reserves the right to discharge the candidate at any stage without notice, even after selection.
  • Canvassing in any form shall disqualify the candidate.
  • No Travelling Allowance/Daily Allowance shall be paid to the candidates.
  • It is not obligatory for the employer to offer any employment to any apprentice after completion of training.
  • The decision of the Railway administration in all matters relating to eligibility, acceptance or rejection of applications and mode of selection shall be final.
  • No correspondence in respect of the applications submitted shall be entertained or replied by this office to any individual or organization.
  • For any query, contact: Mo. No. 9752876639 during office hours 10:00 am to 18:00 pm (Monday to Friday, working days only).

Applicants within the 15 to 24 years age group and possessing a 10th Pass with minimum 50% marks along with ITI in relevant trade can apply online from 05 May 2026 to 04 June 2026. Online applications are invited for engagement as Trade Apprentices in two establishments - DRM Office, Raipur Division (Estt. No. E05202200048) and Wagon Repair Shop, Raipur (Estt. No. E11152200001). A grand total of 1644 vacancies (1363 in Raipur Division + 281 in Wagon Repair Shop) have been announced across multiple designated trades. Online applications are accepted exclusively through the National Apprenticeship Training Portal at https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in from 05.05.2026 to 04.06.2026 (till 23:59 hrs). No physical copy of the application is required to be sent to the office. Candidates are advised to check their eligibility carefully before applying to avoid disappointment at a later stage.

SECR Raipur Division Act Apprentice Vacancy 2026-27

A total of 1644 vacancies have been notified across two establishments for multiple designated ITI trades. The vacancies are distributed across reservation categories - UR (Unreserved), EWS (Economically Weaker Section), OBC (Other Backward Classes), SC (Scheduled Caste), and ST (Scheduled Tribe).

Horizontal reservations are also provided for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) and Ex-Servicemen (Ex.SM). The Fitter and Electrician trades have the highest number of vacancies in the Raipur Division, while Fitter and Welder dominate the Wagon Repair Shop vacancies. SECR Raipur Division Act Apprentice Eligibility Criteria 2026-27

Candidates must fulfil both the educational/technical qualification requirements and the age limit to be eligible for this recruitment. The educational qualifications are mandatory and must have already been obtained before the date of notification - candidates appearing in qualifying examinations or awaiting results are not eligible.

The minimum 10th class marks of 50% aggregate is mandatory and the fraction will be applied strictly (e.g., 49.9% will be treated as less than 50%).

ITI qualification must be in the relevant trade from a recognised institution. Candidates are not required to wait for their ITI marksheet if already passed - they must have the actual pass certificate in hand at the time of application. Council Registration (NCVT/SCVT) of the ITI is also required to be verified during document verification.

01 UR 05-05-2002 to 05-05-2011
02 SC 05-05-1997 to 05-05-2011
03 ST 05-05-1997 to 05-05-2011
04 OBC 05-05-1999 to 05-05-2011
05 PwBD / Ex-Servicemen 05-05-1992 to 05-05-2011

Age Relaxation:

Category Relaxation
SC / ST 5 years
OBC 3 years
Ex-Servicemen 10 years
PwBD 10 years

SECR Raipur Division Act Apprentice Application Fee 2026-27

The official notification does not specify any application fee for this Act Apprentice recruitment. This is consistent with the standard practice for apprenticeship recruitment under the Apprentices Act-1961 conducted by Indian Railway divisions through the National Apprenticeship Training portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in).

No Travelling Allowance (TA) or Daily Allowance (DA) will be paid to candidates for any stage of this recruitment process, including document verification.

Category Application Fee
All Categories NIL (No fee mentioned)

SECR Raipur Division Act Apprentice Selection Process 2026-27

The selection of candidates for Act Apprenticeship in SECR Raipur Division and Wagon Repair Shop, Raipur is based purely on merit, with no written examination. The merit list is prepared by taking the average of the percentage marks obtained in both the 10th (Matriculation) examination and the ITI examination, giving equal weightage to both (as per Railway Establishment Rule 201/2017).

The minimum eligibility score for 10th is 50% aggregate. Candidates who qualify in the merit list will be called for document verification, where all original certificates must be produced. If any discrepancy is found between the uploaded documents and originals, or if original documents are not produced, the candidature will be cancelled.

The Railway Administration's decision in all matters relating to eligibility, acceptance or rejection of applications, and mode of selection is final and binding.

Merit List Preparation Formula:

Component Weightage
10th (Matriculation) Marks Percentage 50%
ITI Marks Percentage 50%
Basis Average of both (equal weightage)

Post-Selection Requirements:

Requirement Details
Medical Examination Medical certificate required at document verification stage as per Apprenticeship Act 1961 and Apprenticeship Rule 1992; must be signed by Government Authorized Doctor (GAZ) not below rank of Asstt. Surgeon
Contract Selected candidate (or guardian if minor) must enter into a contract of apprenticeship with the employer
Training Period 1 year for each trade, with stipend as per Railway Board rules

How to Apply for SECR Raipur Division Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27

The application process is entirely online through the National Apprenticeship India portal. No physical copy of the application is to be sent to the DRM Office, Raipur Division, or Wagon Repair Shop, Raipur. Candidates must register on the portal, fill in all required details accurately, and upload soft/scanned copies of their recent passport-size colour photograph, signature, and all necessary documents pertaining to educational and technical qualification.

SC/ST/OBC candidates must also upload their recent caste certificate issued by the competent authority in the prescribed format and verify their Aadhaar on the portal. OBC candidates must additionally upload a Non-Creamy Layer Certificate - the OBC certificate must not be older than one year from the closing date of this notification.

PwBD candidates must upload a disability certificate from a Medical Board constituted by the Central or State Government certifying not less than 40% disability. EWS candidates must produce their EWS certificate as per sample Annexure-E at the time of document verification.

  • Apply Online at: https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in
  • Application Window: 05/05/2026 to 04/06/2026 (till 23:59 hrs)
  • Upload: Passport-size colour photograph, scanned signature, 10th marksheet, ITI certificate, Caste/EWS/PwBD certificate (as applicable), Aadhaar verification
  • SC/ST/OBC candidates must upload recent caste certificate on the portal and verify Aadhaar
  • No physical application to be sent to the office

Important Instructions for Candidates

Candidates must read all instructions carefully before applying. Applications must be submitted only through the official portal - no other mode of application will be accepted. Canvassing in any form will immediately disqualify the candidate.

No Travelling Allowance or Daily Allowance will be paid to candidates for any stage of the recruitment. If at any stage it is found that a candidate has furnished wrong, fake, or false certificates, the Railway Administration reserves the right to discharge the candidate without notice - even after selection for training.

  • Apply online only at https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in
  • No physical copy of the application to be sent to the DRM Office or Wagon Repair Shop.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification.
  • Candidates must already hold the required qualifications on the date of notification (05.05.2026) - candidates awaiting results are not eligible.
  • All original documents must be produced at the time of document verification.
  • No TA/DA will be paid for any stage of the recruitment.
  • The Railway Administration's decision in all matters is final and binding.

How to Raise Objection Against PSSSB Group D Answer Key 2026 ?

If you disagree with any answer in the provisional key, follow these steps:

  4. Prepare Demand Draft (DD) of ₹50 per objection, drawn in favour of Secretary, PSSSB
  5. Send scanned copies via email (official email to be announced) and original hard copy by Speed Post / hand delivery to: Secretary, Subordinate Services Selection Board, Punjab, Forest Complex, Sector-68, SAS Nagar, Mohali – 160062

UPSC Marksheet 2025 – Download PDF & Check Scores

The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has released the UPSC Marksheet 2025 for the Civil Services Examination (CSE). Candidates who appeared for the CSE 2025 (Mains and Personality Test) can now check and download their marks from the official website upsc.gov.in.

FOLLOW US

The final result was declared on March 6, 2026, recommending 958 candidates (including toppers and category-wise selections). The marksheet includes marks in Mains (out of 1750) and Interview (out of 275), totaling 2025 marks. It is available as a PDF for recommended candidates, with individual detailed marksheets accessible via login for all appeared candidates.

How to Download UPSC Marksheet 2025 ?

Follow these simple steps to download your marksheet:

  1. Visit the official website: upsc.gov.in
  2. Navigate to the "Examinations" or "Marksheet" section
  3. Click on "Marksheet - Civil Services Examination 2025" link
  4. Log in using your Roll Number and Date of Birth
  5. Your marksheet with detailed scores (Mains papers, Interview, total) will appear
  6. Download and save the PDF for future reference

How to Calculate / Understand Your Score Using UPSC Marksheet 2025 ?

Total Score = Mains Marks (out of 1750) + Interview Marks (out of 275)

Marking Scheme Overview:

  • Mains: 7 papers (GS I-IV, Optional I-II, Essay) contributing to 1750 marks
  • Interview/Personality Test: 275 marks
  • Prelims marks are qualifying only and not included in final tally
